title: Not All Features Are Created Equal: A Mechanistic Study of Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models combine perception, language, and motor control in a single architecture, yet how they translate multimodal inputs into actions remains poorly understood. We apply activation injection, sparse autoencoders (SAEs), and linear probes to six models spanning 80M--7B parameters across 394,000+ rollout episodes on four benchmarks. The visual pathway dominates action generation across all architectures: injecting baseline activations into null-prompt episodes recovers near-identical behavior, while cross-task injection steers robots toward source-task positions (99.8\% of X-VLA episodes align with the source trajectory), exposing spatially bound motor programs tied to scene coordinates rather than abstract task representations. Language sensitivity depends on task structure, not model design: when visual context uniquely specifies the task, language is ignored; when multiple goals share a scene, language becomes essential (X-VLA \texttt{libero\_goal}: 94\%$\to$10\% under wrong prompts vs.\ \texttt{libero\_object}: 60--100\% regardless). In all three multi-pathway architectures (\pizhalf{}, SmolVLA, GR00T), expert pathways encode motor programs while VLM pathways encode goal semantics ($2\times$ greater behavioral displacement from expert injection), and subspace injection confirms these occupy separable activation subspaces. Per-token SAE processing is essential for action fidelity on most architectures, though mean-pooling improves fidelity on X-VLA. Contrastive identification recovers 82+ manipulation concepts, and causal ablation reveals sensitivity spanning 28--92\% zero-effect rates independent of representation width. We release \textbf{Action Atlas} (https://action-atlas.com) for interactive exploration of VLA representations across all six models.
tldr: This paper investigates how Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models translate multimodal inputs into actions, revealing the dominance of the visual pathway for action generation and the task-dependent role of language. They further identify separable activation subspaces for expert and VLM pathways.
tldr_zh: 该论文研究了视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型如何将多模态输入转化为动作，揭示了视觉路径在动作生成中的主导地位以及语言的任务依赖性。他们还确定了专家和VLM路径的可分离激活子空间。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在单一架构中结合了感知、语言和运动控制，但它们如何将多模态输入转化为动作仍然知之甚少。我们在四个基准测试上，针对六个模型（参数规模从8千万到70亿，覆盖超过 39.4 万个 rollout  episode）应用了激活注入、稀疏自编码器（SAE）和线性探针。视觉通路在所有架构的动作生成中占据主导地位：将基线激活注入到空提示 episode 中可恢复几乎相同的行为，而跨任务注入会将机器人引导至源任务位置（99.8%的 X-VLA episode 与源轨迹对齐），揭示了与场景坐标相关的、空间约束的运动程序，而非抽象的任务表示。语言敏感性取决于任务结构，而非模型设计：当视觉上下文唯一指定了任务时，语言被忽略；当多个目标共享一个场景时，语言变得至关重要（在错误的提示下，X-VLA \texttt{libero\_goal}: 94%$\to$10% vs. \texttt{libero\_object}: 60--100% ）。在所有三个多通路架构（\pizhalf{}、SmolVLA、GR00T）中，专家通路编码运动程序，而 VLM通路编码目标语义（来自动注专家通路的扰动引起的行为位移是原来的 2 倍），子空间注入证实了这些通路占据着可分离的激活子空间。对于大多数架构来说，逐token SAE 处理对于动作保真度至关重要，而平均池化则提高了 X-VLA 的保真度。对比识别恢复了 82 个以上的操作概念，因果消融显示敏感性跨越了 28-92% 的零效应率，且与表示宽度无关。我们发布了 \textbf{Action Atlas} (https://action-atlas.com)，用于对所有六个模型的 VLA 表示进行交互式探索。

title: From Inference Efficiency to Embodied Efficiency: Revisiting Efficiency Metrics for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently enabled embodied agents to perform increasingly complex tasks by jointly reasoning over visual, linguistic, and motor modalities. However, we find that the prevailing notion of ``efficiency'' in current VLA research, characterized by parameters, FLOPs, or token decoding throughput, does not reflect actual performance on robotic platforms. In real-world execution, efficiency is determined by system-level embodied behaviors such as task completion time, trajectory smoothness, cumulative joint rotation, and motion energy. Through controlled studies across model compression, token sparsification, and action sequence compression, we make several observations that challenge common assumptions. (1) Methods that reduce computation under conventional metrics often increase end-to-end execution cost or degrade motion quality, despite maintaining task success rates. (2) System-level embodied efficiency metrics reveal performance differences in the learned action policies that remain hidden under conventional evaluations. (3) Common adaptation methods such as in-context prompting or supervised fine-tuning show only mild and metric-specific improvements in embodied efficiency. While these methods can reduce targeted embodied-efficiency metrics such as jerk or action rate, the resulting gains may come with trade-offs in other metrics, such as longer completion time. Taken together, our results suggest that conventional inference efficiency metrics can overlook important aspects of embodied execution. Incorporating embodied efficiency provides a more complete view of policy behavior and practical performance, enabling fairer and more comprehensive comparisons of VLA models.
tldr: This paper argues that conventional efficiency metrics for Vision-Language-Action models (parameters, FLOPs) are poor indicators of real-world robotic performance (task completion time, motion quality). It identifies discrepancies and trade-offs between the two sets of metrics.
tldr_zh: 本文指出，视觉-语言-动作模型 (VLA) 的传统效率指标（参数量、FLOPs）不能很好地反映真实机器人性能（任务完成时间、运动质量）。 它指出了这两组指标之间的差异和权衡。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型最近使得具身智能体能够通过联合推理视觉、语言和运动模态来执行日益复杂的任务。然而，我们发现当前VLA研究中普遍存在的关于“效率”的概念，其特征在于参数量、FLOPs或token解码吞吐量，并不能反映在机器人平台上实际的性能表现。在真实世界的执行中，效率是由系统级的具身行为决定的，例如任务完成时间、轨迹平滑度、累积关节旋转和运动能量。通过对模型压缩、token稀疏化和动作序列压缩的对照研究，我们得到了一些挑战现有常见假设的结论。(1) 在传统指标下降低计算量的方法通常会增加端到端执行成本或降低运动质量，尽管保持了任务成功率。(2) 系统级的具身效率指标揭示了在传统评估中仍然隐藏的学习到的动作策略的性能差异。(3) 诸如上下文提示或监督微调等常见的适应方法在具身效率方面仅表现出轻微且指标特定的改进。虽然这些方法可以降低诸如加加速度或动作频率等有针对性的具身效率指标，但由此带来的收益可能会以其他指标（例如更长的完成时间）的权衡为代价。综上所述，我们的结果表明，传统的推理效率指标可能会忽略具身执行的重要方面。纳入具身效率可以更全面地了解策略行为和实际性能，从而能够对VLA模型进行更公平、更全面的比较。

title: Scaling Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning for Robot VLAs with Generative 3D Worlds
summary: The strong performance of large vision-language models (VLMs) trained with reinforcement learning (RL) has motivated similar approaches for fine-tuning vision-language-action (VLA) models in robotics. Many recent works fine-tune VLAs directly in the real world to avoid addressing the sim-to-real gap. While real-world RL circumvents sim-to-real issues, it inherently limits the generality of the resulting VLA, as scaling scene and object diversity in the physical world is prohibitively difficult. This leads to the paradoxical outcome of transforming a broadly pretrained model into an overfitted, scene-specific policy. Training in simulation can instead provide access to diverse scenes, but designing those scenes is also costly. In this work, we show that VLAs can be RL fine-tuned without sacrificing generality and with reduced labor by leveraging 3D world generative models. Using these models together with a language-driven scene designer, we generate hundreds of diverse interactive scenes containing unique objects and backgrounds, enabling scalable and highly parallel policy learning. Starting from a pretrained imitation baseline, our approach increases simulation success from 9.7% to 79.8% while achieving a 1.25$\times$ speedup in task completion time. We further demonstrate successful sim-to-real transfer enabled by the quality of the generated digital twins together with domain randomization, improving real-world success from 21.7% to 75% and achieving a 1.13$\times$ speedup. Finally, we further highlight the benefits of leveraging the effectively unlimited data from 3D world generative models through an ablation study showing that increasing scene diversity directly improves zero-shot generalization.
tldr: This paper demonstrates a method for scaling sim-to-real reinforcement learning for robot VLAs by using generative 3D world models to create diverse training environments, leading to improved simulation and real-world performance.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种通过使用生成式3D世界模型创建多样化训练环境来扩展机器人VLA的sim-to-real强化学习的方法，从而提高了仿真和现实世界的性能。
summary_zh: 通过强化学习（RL）训练的大型视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的强大性能推动了类似的方法，用于在机器人技术中微调视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型。 近期许多工作直接在真实世界中微调VLA模型，以避免解决仿真到现实（sim-to-real）的差距。 尽管真实世界强化学习规避了仿真到现实的问题，但它本质上限制了所得到的VLA模型的泛化性，因为在物理世界中扩展场景和对象的多样性非常困难。 这导致了一个自相矛盾的结果：将一个广泛预训练的模型转化为一个过拟合的、特定于场景的策略。 相反，在仿真中训练可以提供对各种场景的访问，但设计这些场景的成本也很高。 在这项工作中，我们表明，通过利用3D世界生成模型，可以对VLA模型进行强化学习微调，而无需牺牲泛化性并减少人工成本。 通过将这些模型与语言驱动的场景设计器一起使用，我们生成了数百个包含独特对象和背景的多样化交互场景，从而实现了可扩展且高度并行的策略学习。 从预训练的模仿基线开始，我们的方法将仿真成功率从9.7%提高到79.8%，同时任务完成时间加速了1.25倍。 我们进一步展示了由生成的数字孪生的质量以及领域随机化所实现的成功的仿真到现实转移，将真实世界成功率从21.7%提高到75%，并实现了1.13倍的加速。 最后，我们通过一项消融研究进一步强调了利用来自3D世界生成模型的有效无限数据的益处，表明增加场景多样性可以直接提高零样本泛化能力。

title: DriveVLM-RL: Neuroscience-Inspired Reinforcement Learning with Vision-Language Models for Safe and Deployable Autonomous Driving
summary: Ensuring safe decision-making in autonomous vehicles remains a fundamental challenge despite rapid advances in end-to-end learning approaches. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods rely on manually engineered rewards or sparse collision signals, which fail to capture the rich contextual understanding required for safe driving and make unsafe exploration unavoidable in real-world settings. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) offer promising semantic understanding capabilities; however, their high inference latency and susceptibility to hallucination hinder direct application to real-time vehicle control. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DriveVLM-RL, a neuroscience-inspired framework that integrates VLMs into RL through a dual-pathway architecture for safe and deployable autonomous driving. The framework decomposes semantic reward learning into a Static Pathway for continuous spatial safety assessment using CLIP-based contrasting language goals, and a Dynamic Pathway for attention-gated multi-frame semantic risk reasoning using a lightweight detector and a large VLM. A hierarchical reward synthesis mechanism fuses semantic signals with vehicle states, while an asynchronous training pipeline decouples expensive VLM inference from environment interaction. All VLM components are used only during offline training and are removed at deployment, ensuring real-time feasibility. Experiments in the CARLA simulator show significant improvements in collision avoidance, task success, and generalization across diverse traffic scenarios, including strong robustness under settings without explicit collision penalties. These results demonstrate that DriveVLM-RL provides a practical paradigm for integrating foundation models into autonomous driving without compromising real-time feasibility. Demo video and code are available at: https://zilin-huang.github.io/DriveVLM-RL-website/
tldr: DriveVLM-RL uses a neuroscience-inspired, dual-pathway architecture to integrate VLMs into RL for safe autonomous driving in CARLA, removing VLMs at deployment for real-time feasibility.
tldr_zh: DriveVLM-RL采用受神经科学启发的双路径架构，将VLM集成到RL中，以实现CARLA中安全的自动驾驶，并在部署时移除VLM以确保实时的可行性。
summary_zh: 尽管端到端学习方法取得了快速进展，但确保自动驾驶汽车的安全决策仍然是一项根本性的挑战。传统的强化学习（RL）方法依赖于手动设计的奖励或稀疏的碰撞信号，这无法捕捉安全驾驶所需的丰富的上下文理解，并使得在真实世界环境中的不安全探索不可避免。近期的视觉-语言模型（VLMs）展现了有希望的语义理解能力；然而，其高推理延迟和易受幻觉影响的特性阻碍了其直接应用于实时车辆控制。为了解决这些局限性，本文提出了一种受神经科学启发的框架DriveVLM-RL，该框架通过双通路架构将VLMs集成到RL中，从而实现安全且可部署的自动驾驶。该框架将语义奖励学习分解为：静态通路，用于使用基于CLIP的对比语言目标进行连续的空间安全评估；以及动态通路，用于使用轻量级检测器和大型VLM进行注意力门控的多帧语义风险推理。一种分层奖励合成机制将语义信号与车辆状态融合，而异步训练管线将昂贵的VLM推理与环境交互解耦。所有VLM组件仅在离线训练期间使用，并在部署时移除，从而确保实时可行性。在CARLA模拟器中的实验表明，在碰撞规避、任务成功率以及跨各种交通场景的泛化方面都有显著改进，包括在没有明确碰撞惩罚的设置下也表现出强大的鲁棒性。这些结果表明，DriveVLM-RL为将基础模型集成到自动驾驶中提供了一种实用的范例，而不会影响实时可行性。演示视频和代码可在以下网址获取：https://zilin-huang.github.io/DriveVLM-RL-website/

title: AcceRL: A Distributed Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning and World Model Framework for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) for large-scale Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models faces significant challenges in computational efficiency and data acquisition. We propose AcceRL, a fully asynchronous and decoupled RL framework designed to eliminate synchronization barriers by physically isolating training, inference, and rollouts. Crucially, AcceRL is the first to integrate a plug-and-play, trainable world model into a distributed asynchronous RL pipeline to generate virtual experiences. Experiments on the LIBERO benchmark demonstrate that AcceRL achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance. Systematically, it exhibits super-linear scaling in throughput and highly efficient hardware utilization. Algorithmically, the world-model-augmented variant delivers unprecedented sample efficiency and robust training stability in complex control tasks.
tldr: AcceRL is a novel asynchronous and decoupled RL framework with a trainable world model for VLA tasks, achieving SOTA performance on LIBERO with super-linear scaling and improved sample efficiency.
tldr_zh: AcceRL 是一个用于视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 任务的新型异步解耦强化学习框架，具有可训练的世界模型。该框架在 LIBERO 上实现了 SOTA 性能，具有超线性扩展和更高的样本效率。
summary_zh: 针对大规模视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的强化学习（RL）在计算效率和数据获取方面面临着严峻的挑战。我们提出AcceRL，一种完全异步且解耦的RL框架，旨在通过物理隔离训练、推理和 rollout 来消除同步壁垒。至关重要的是，AcceRL是首个将即插即用、可训练的世界模型集成到分布式异步RL管道中，以生成虚拟经验的框架。在LIBERO基准测试上的实验表明，AcceRL实现了最先进（SOTA）的性能。从系统层面来看，AcceRL表现出超线性的吞吐量扩展和高效的硬件利用率。从算法层面来看，世界模型增强的变体在复杂的控制任务中提供了前所未有的样本效率和稳健的训练稳定性。

title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding
summary: While Multimodal Large Language Models demonstrate impressive semantic capabilities, they often suffer from spatial blindness, struggling with fine-grained geometric reasoning and physical dynamics. Existing solutions typically rely on explicit 3D modalities or complex geometric scaffolding, which are limited by data scarcity and generalization challenges. In this work, we propose a paradigm shift by leveraging the implicit spatial prior within large-scale video generation models. We posit that to synthesize temporally coherent videos, these models inherently learn robust 3D structural priors and physical laws. We introduce VEGA-3D (Video Extracted Generative Awareness), a plug-and-play framework that repurposes a pre-trained video diffusion model as a Latent World Simulator. By extracting spatiotemporal features from intermediate noise levels and integrating them with semantic representations via a token-level adaptive gated fusion mechanism, we enrich MLLMs with dense geometric cues without explicit 3D supervision. Extensive experiments across 3D scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, validating that generative priors provide a scalable foundation for physical-world understanding. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/VEGA-3D.
tldr: VEGA-3D leverages implicit 3D priors learned by video generation models to enhance MLLMs' spatial reasoning capabilities, demonstrating improvements in scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation without explicit 3D supervision.
tldr_zh: VEGA-3D利用视频生成模型学习到的隐式3D先验来增强MLLM的空间推理能力，无需显式的3D监督即可在场景理解、空间推理和具身操作方面实现改进。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型在展示出令人印象深刻的语义能力的同时，常常遭受空间盲症的困扰，难以进行细粒度的几何推理和物理动力学建模。 现有的解决方案通常依赖于显式的 3D 模型或复杂的几何支架，但受限于数据稀缺和泛化挑战。 在这项工作中，我们提出一种范式转变，即利用大规模视频生成模型中蕴含的隐式空间先验。 我们假设，为了合成时间上连贯的视频，这些模型必然学习了鲁棒的 3D 结构先验和物理定律。 我们引入了 VEGA-3D（视频提取生成感知），这是一个即插即用的框架，它将预训练的视频扩散模型重新用作潜在世界模拟器。 通过从中间噪声级别中提取时空特征，并使用令牌级自适应门控融合机制将其与语义表示相结合，我们丰富了 MLLM，使其具有密集的几何线索，而无需显式的 3D 监督。 在 3D 场景理解、空间推理和具身操作基准上的大量实验表明，我们的方法优于最先进的基线，验证了生成先验为物理世界理解提供了一个可扩展的基础。 代码可在 https://github.com/H-EmbodVis/VEGA-3D 公开获取。

title: NavTrust: Benchmarking Trustworthiness for Embodied Navigation
summary: There are two major categories of embodied navigation: Vision-Language Navigation (VLN), where agents navigate by following natural language instructions; and Object-Goal Navigation (OGN), where agents navigate to a specified target object. However, existing work primarily evaluates model performance under nominal conditions, overlooking the potential corruptions that arise in real-world settings. To address this gap, we present NavTrust, a unified benchmark that systematically corrupts input modalities, including RGB, depth, and instructions, in realistic scenarios and evaluates their impact on navigation performance. To our best knowledge, NavTrust is the first benchmark that exposes embodied navigation agents to diverse RGB-Depth corruptions and instruction variations in a unified framework. Our extensive evaluation of seven state-of-the-art approaches reveals substantial performance degradation under realistic corruptions, which highlights critical robustness gaps and provides a roadmap toward more trustworthy embodied navigation systems. Furthermore, we systematically evaluate four distinct mitigation strategies to enhance robustness against RGB-Depth and instructions corruptions. Our base models include Uni-NaVid and ETPNav. We deployed them on a real mobile robot and observed improved robustness to corruptions. The project website is: https://navtrust.github.io.
tldr: The paper introduces NavTrust, a new benchmark for evaluating the robustness of embodied navigation agents under various realistic corruptions of RGB, depth, and language inputs, and provides mitigation strategies.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了NavTrust，一个新的基准测试，用于评估具身导航智能体在各种真实的RGB、深度和语言输入损坏下的鲁棒性，并提供了缓解策略。
summary_zh: 具身导航主要分为两大类：视觉-语言导航（VLN），其中智能体通过遵循自然语言指令进行导航；以及目标物体导航（OGN），其中智能体导航至指定的目标物体。然而，现有工作主要在理想条件下评估模型性能，忽略了现实环境中可能出现的潜在损坏。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了NavTrust，一个统一的基准，它在真实场景中系统性地损坏输入模态，包括RGB、深度和指令，并评估它们对导航性能的影响。据我们所知，NavTrust是第一个在统一框架内将具身导航智能体暴露于各种RGB-Depth损坏和指令变化的基准。我们对七种最先进方法的广泛评估表明，在真实的损坏下，性能出现显著下降，这凸显了关键的鲁棒性差距，并为实现更值得信赖的具身导航系统提供了路线图。此外，我们系统性地评估了四种不同的缓解策略，以增强针对RGB-Depth和指令损坏的鲁棒性。我们的基础模型包括Uni-NaVid和ETPNav。我们将它们部署在真实的移动机器人上，并观察到对损坏的鲁棒性有所提高。项目网站是：https://navtrust.github.io。

title: OmniVTA: Visuo-Tactile World Modeling for Contact-Rich Robotic Manipulation
summary: Contact-rich manipulation tasks, such as wiping and assembly, require accurate perception of contact forces, friction changes, and state transitions that cannot be reliably inferred from vision alone. Despite growing interest in visuo-tactile manipulation, progress is constrained by two persistent limitations: existing datasets are small in scale and narrow in task coverage, and current methods treat tactile signals as passive observations rather than using them to model contact dynamics or enable closed-loop control explicitly. In this paper, we present \textbf{OmniViTac}, a large-scale visuo-tactile-action dataset comprising $21{,}000+$ trajectories across $86$ tasks and $100+$ objects, organized into six physics-grounded interaction patterns. Building on this dataset, we propose \textbf{OmniVTA}, a world-model-based visuo-tactile manipulation framework that integrates four tightly coupled modules: a self-supervised tactile encoder, a two-stream visuo-tactile world model for predicting short-horizon contact evolution, a contact-aware fusion policy for action generation, and a 60Hz reflexive controller that corrects deviations between predicted and observed tactile signals in a closed loop. Real-robot experiments across all six interaction categories show that OmniVTA outperforms existing methods and generalizes well to unseen objects and geometric configurations, confirming the value of combining predictive contact modeling with high-frequency tactile feedback for contact-rich manipulation. All data, models, and code will be made publicly available on the project website at https://mrsecant.github.io/OmniVTA.
tldr: This paper introduces OmniVTA, a large-scale visuo-tactile dataset and a corresponding world model-based framework for contact-rich robotic manipulation, demonstrating improved performance through predictive contact modeling and high-frequency tactile feedback.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了OmniVTA，一个大规模视觉触觉数据集以及相应的基于世界模型的框架，用于富接触机器人操作，通过预测性接触建模和高频触觉反馈展示了性能的提升。
summary_zh: 诸如擦拭和组装等接触型操作任务需要对接触力、摩擦变化和状态转换进行精确感知，而这些不能仅从视觉信息中可靠地推断出来。尽管人们对视觉-触觉操作越来越感兴趣，但进展受到两个长期存在的限制：现有数据集规模小且任务覆盖范围窄，并且当前方法将触觉信号视为被动观察，而不是使用它们来建模接触动力学或显式地实现闭环控制。在本文中，我们提出了\textbf{OmniViTac}，这是一个大规模的视觉-触觉-动作数据集，包含跨86个任务和100多个对象的21,000+条轨迹，并组织成六种基于物理的交互模式。在此数据集的基础上，我们提出了\textbf{OmniVTA}，这是一个基于世界模型的视觉-触觉操作框架，集成了四个紧密耦合的模块：一个自监督触觉编码器，一个用于预测短时程接触演化的双流视觉-触觉世界模型，一个用于动作生成的接触感知融合策略，以及一个以60Hz运行的反射控制器，用于闭环校正预测和观察到的触觉信号之间的偏差。涵盖所有六种交互类别的真实机器人实验表明，OmniVTA优于现有方法，并且能够很好地泛化到未见过的对象和几何配置，这证实了将预测性接触建模与高频触觉反馈相结合对于接触型操作的价值。所有数据、模型和代码将在项目网站https://mrsecant.github.io/OmniVTA上公开发布。

title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
summary: Real-time execution is crucial for deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in the physical world. Existing asynchronous inference methods primarily optimize trajectory smoothness, but neglect the critical latency in reacting to environmental changes. By rethinking the notion of reaction in action chunking policies, this paper presents a systematic analysis of the factors governing reaction time. We show that reaction time follows a uniform distribution determined jointly by the Time to First Action (TTFA) and the execution horizon. Moreover, we reveal that the standard practice of applying a constant schedule in flow-based VLAs can be inefficient and forces the system to complete all sampling steps before any movement can start, forming the bottleneck in reaction latency. To overcome this issue, we propose Fast Action Sampling for ImmediaTE Reaction (FASTER). By introducing a Horizon-Aware Schedule, FASTER adaptively prioritizes near-term actions during flow sampling, compressing the denoising of the immediate reaction by tenfold (e.g., in $π_{0.5}$ and X-VLA) into a single step, while preserving the quality of long-horizon trajectory. Coupled with a streaming client-server pipeline, FASTER substantially reduces the effective reaction latency on real robots, especially when deployed on consumer-grade GPUs. Real-world experiments, including a highly dynamic table tennis task, prove that FASTER unlocks unprecedented real-time responsiveness for generalist policies, enabling rapid generation of accurate and smooth trajectories.
tldr: This paper introduces FASTER, a novel approach to improve the real-time responsiveness of Vision-Language-Action models by adaptively prioritizing near-term actions in flow sampling, significantly reducing reaction latency, particularly on consumer-grade GPUs, and demonstrating it in dynamic real-world tasks such as table tennis.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为FASTER 的新方法，通过自适应地优先处理流程采样中的近期行动来提高视觉-语言-动作模型的实时响应能力，显着降低了反应延迟，尤其是在消费级 GPU 上，并在动态的现实任务（如乒乓球）中进行了演示。
summary_zh: 实时执行对于在物理世界中部署视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型至关重要。现有的异步推理方法主要优化轨迹平滑性，但忽略了对环境变化做出反应的关键延迟。通过重新思考动作分块策略中反应的概念，本文系统分析了决定反应时间的因素。我们表明，反应时间服从一个由首次动作时间 (TTFA) 和执行范围共同决定的均匀分布。此外，我们揭示了在基于流的 VLA 中应用恒定调度的常见做法可能效率低下，并且迫使系统在开始任何移动之前完成所有采样步骤，从而形成反应延迟的瓶颈。为了克服这个问题，我们提出了一种用于立即响应的快速动作采样 (FASTER)。通过引入一种范围感知调度，FASTER 自适应地优先考虑流采样期间的近期动作，将立即反应的去噪过程压缩十倍（例如，在 $π_{0.5}$ 和 X-VLA 中）到一个步骤中，同时保持长范围轨迹的质量。结合流式客户端-服务器流水线，FASTER 大大降低了真实机器人上的有效反应延迟，尤其是在部署于消费级 GPU 上时。包括高度动态的乒乓球任务在内的真实世界实验证明，FASTER 为通用策略解锁了前所未有的实时响应能力，从而能够快速生成准确和平滑的轨迹。

title: Sparse Autoencoders Reveal Interpretable and Steerable Features in VLA Models
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a promising approach for general-purpose robot manipulation. However, their generalization is inconsistent: while these models can perform impressively in some settings, fine-tuned variants often fail on novel objects, scenes, and instructions. We apply mechanistic interpretability techniques to better understand the inner workings of VLA models. To probe internal representations, we train Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) on hidden layer activations of the VLA. SAEs learn a sparse dictionary whose features act as a compact, interpretable basis for the model's computation. We find that the large majority of extracted SAE features correspond to memorized sequences from specific training demonstrations. However, some features correspond to interpretable, general, and steerable motion primitives and semantic properties, offering a promising glimpse toward VLA generalizability. We propose a metric to categorize features according to whether they represent generalizable transferable primitives or episode-specific memorization. We validate these findings through steering experiments on the LIBERO benchmark. We show that individual SAE features causally influence robot behavior. Steering general features induces behaviors consistent with their semantic meaning and can be applied across tasks and scenes. This work provides the first mechanistic evidence that VLAs can learn generalizable features across tasks and scenes. We observe that supervised fine-tuning on small robotics datasets disproportionately amplifies memorization. In contrast, training on larger, more diverse datasets (e.g., DROID) or using knowledge insulation promotes more general features. We provide an open-source codebase and user-friendly interface for activation collection, SAE training, and feature steering. Our project page is located at http://drvla.github.io
tldr: This paper uses sparse autoencoders to analyze Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, revealing both memorized sequences and generalizable motion primitives. The study demonstrates that steering these general features can induce meaningful behaviors across tasks and scenes, suggesting a path toward better VLA generalizability.
tldr_zh: 本文利用稀疏自编码器分析视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型，揭示了记忆序列和可泛化的运动原语。研究表明，引导这些通用特征可以跨任务和场景引发有意义的行为，为VLA更好地泛化提供了方向。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型已成为一种有前景的通用机器人操作方法。然而，它们的泛化能力并不稳定：虽然这些模型在某些设置中表现出色，但微调的变体通常在新的物体、场景和指令上失败。我们应用机械可解释性技术来更好地理解VLA模型的内部运作机制。为了探究内部表征，我们在VLA的隐藏层激活上训练稀疏自动编码器 (SAE)。SAE学习一个稀疏字典，其特征作为模型计算的紧凑且可解释的基础。我们发现，绝大多数提取的SAE特征都对应于特定训练演示中记忆的序列。然而，一些特征对应于可解释、通用且可操纵的运动原语和语义属性，为VLA的泛化能力提供了一个有希望的方向。我们提出了一种度量标准，用于根据特征表示可泛化的可迁移原语还是特定于情节的记忆进行分类。我们通过在LIBERO基准上进行的操控实验验证了这些发现。我们表明，单个SAE特征会因果地影响机器人行为。操控通用特征会产生与其语义含义一致的行为，并且可以跨任务和场景应用。这项工作提供了第一个机械证据，表明VLA可以学习跨任务和场景的泛化特征。我们观察到，在小型机器人数据集上进行监督微调，不成比例地放大了记忆。相比之下，在更大、更多样化的数据集（例如，DROID）上进行训练或使用知识隔离可以促进更通用的特征。我们提供了一个开源代码库和用户友好的界面，用于激活收集、SAE训练和特征操控。我们的项目页面位于http://drvla.github.io

title: Meanings and Measurements: Multi-Agent Probabilistic Grounding for Vision-Language Navigation
summary: Robots collaborating with humans must convert natural language goals into actionable, physically grounded decisions. For example, executing a command such as "go two meters to the right of the fridge" requires grounding semantic references, spatial relations, and metric constraints within a 3D scene. While recent vision language models (VLMs) demonstrate strong semantic grounding capabilities, they are not explicitly designed to reason about metric constraints in physically defined spaces. In this work, we empirically demonstrate that state-of-the-art VLM-based grounding approaches struggle with complex metric-semantic language queries. To address this limitation, we propose MAPG (Multi-Agent Probabilistic Grounding), an agentic framework that decomposes language queries into structured subcomponents and queries a VLM to ground each component. MAPG then probabilistically composes these grounded outputs to produce metrically consistent, actionable decisions in 3D space. We evaluate MAPG on the HM-EQA benchmark and show consistent performance improvements over strong baselines. Furthermore, we introduce a new benchmark, MAPG-Bench, specifically designed to evaluate metric-semantic goal grounding, addressing a gap in existing language grounding evaluations. We also present a real-world robot demonstration showing that MAPG transfers beyond simulation when a structured scene representation is available.
tldr: This paper introduces MAPG, a multi-agent probabilistic grounding framework, to improve vision-language navigation by addressing the limitations of existing VLMs in handling metric constraints within 3D environments. They demonstrate its effectiveness on a new benchmark designed for metric-semantic goal grounding and in a real-world robot demonstration.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了MAPG，一个多智能体概率接地框架，通过解决现有VLM在处理3D环境中度量约束方面的局限性，来改进视觉语言导航。他们在专门为度量语义目标接地设计的新基准测试和真实世界的机器人演示中展示了其有效性。
summary_zh: 与人类协作的机器人必须将自然语言目标转化为可执行、具有物理基础的决策。例如，执行诸如“走到冰箱右侧两米处”的命令需要在三维场景中确定语义引用、空间关系和度量约束。虽然最近的视觉语言模型（VLMs）表现出强大的语义基础能力，但它们并非专门设计用于推理物理定义空间中的度量约束。在这项工作中，我们通过实验证明，最先进的基于VLM的基础方法在处理复杂的度量-语义语言查询时存在困难。为了解决这个限制，我们提出了MAPG（多智能体概率基础），一个智能体框架，它将语言查询分解为结构化的子组件，并查询VLM以确定每个子组件的基础。然后，MAPG 以概率方式组合这些已确定的基础输出，以在三维空间中产生符合度量标准的、可执行的决策。我们在 HM-EQA 基准测试上评估 MAPG，并显示出相对于强大基线的持续性能改进。此外，我们引入了一个新的基准测试 MAPG-Bench，专门用于评估度量-语义目标基础，解决了现有语言基础评估中的一个差距。我们还展示了一个真实世界的机器人演示，表明当结构化的场景表示可用时，MAPG 可以转移到模拟环境之外。

title: GSMem: 3D Gaussian Splatting as Persistent Spatial Memory for Zero-Shot Embodied Exploration and Reasoning
summary: Effective embodied exploration requires agents to accumulate and retain spatial knowledge over time. However, existing scene representations, such as discrete scene graphs or static view-based snapshots, lack \textit{post-hoc re-observability}. If an initial observation misses a target, the resulting memory omission is often irrecoverable. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{GSMem}, a zero-shot embodied exploration and reasoning framework built upon 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). By explicitly parameterizing continuous geometry and dense appearance, 3DGS serves as a persistent spatial memory that endows the agent with \textit{Spatial Recollection}: the ability to render photorealistic novel views from optimal, previously unoccupied viewpoints. To operationalize this, GSMem employs a retrieval mechanism that simultaneously leverages parallel object-level scene graphs and semantic-level language fields. This complementary design robustly localizes target regions, enabling the agent to ``hallucinate'' optimal views for high-fidelity Vision-Language Model (VLM) reasoning. Furthermore, we introduce a hybrid exploration strategy that combines VLM-driven semantic scoring with a 3DGS-based coverage objective, balancing task-aware exploration with geometric coverage. Extensive experiments on embodied question answering and lifelong navigation demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of our framework
tldr: The paper introduces GSMem, a framework leveraging 3D Gaussian Splatting for persistent spatial memory in embodied agents, enabling improved zero-shot exploration and reasoning through spatial recollection and VLM-driven hybrid exploration.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了GSMem，一个利用3D高斯溅射（3D Gaussian Splatting）作为具身智能体持久空间记忆的框架，通过空间回忆（Spatial Recollection）和基于VLM的混合探索，提高了零样本探索和推理能力。
summary_zh: 有效的具身探索要求智能体随时间积累并保持空间知识。然而，现有的场景表示，例如离散场景图或静态的基于视角的快照，缺乏\textit{后验可重观测性}。如果初始观测遗漏了目标，由此产生的记忆遗漏通常是不可恢复的。为了弥合这个差距，我们提出了\textbf{GSMem}，一个基于3D高斯溅射（3DGS）构建的零样本具身探索和推理框架。通过显式地参数化连续几何体和密集外观，3DGS充当持久的空间记忆，赋予智能体\textit{空间回忆}能力：从最佳的、先前未占据的视角渲染照片级逼真的新视角。为了实现这一点，GSMem采用一种检索机制，同时利用并行对象级场景图和语义级语言字段。这种互补设计能够稳健地定位目标区域，使智能体能够“幻化”最佳视角，以进行高保真的视觉-语言模型（VLM）推理。此外，我们引入了一种混合探索策略，该策略将VLM驱动的语义评分与基于3DGS的覆盖目标相结合，从而平衡了任务感知探索和几何覆盖。在具身问答和终身导航方面的大量实验证明了我们框架的鲁棒性和有效性。

title: Articulated-Body Dynamics Network: Dynamics-Grounded Prior for Robot Learning
summary: Recent work in reinforcement learning has shown that incorporating structural priors for articulated robots, such as link connectivity, into policy networks improves learning efficiency. However, dynamics properties, despite their fundamental role in determining how forces and motion propagate through the body, remain largely underexplored as an inductive bias for policy learning. To address this gap, we present the Articulated-Body Dynamics Network (ABD-Net), a novel graph neural network architecture grounded in the computational structure of forward dynamics. Specifically, we adapt the inertia propagation mechanism from the Articulated Body Algorithm, systematically aggregating inertial quantities from child to parent links in a tree-structured manner, while replacing physical quantities with learnable parameters. Embedding ABD-NET into the policy actor enables dynamics-informed representations that capture how actions propagate through the body, leading to efficient and robust policy learning. Through experiments with simulated humanoid, quadruped, and hopper robots, our approach demonstrates increased sample efficiency and generalization to dynamics shifts compared to transformer-based and GNN baselines. We further validate the learned policy on real Unitree G1 and Go2 robots, state-of-the-art humanoid and quadruped platforms, generating dynamic, versatile and robust locomotion behaviors through sim-to-real transfer with real-time inference.
tldr: The paper introduces Articulated-Body Dynamics Network (ABD-Net), a novel graph neural network architecture grounded in forward dynamics, to improve sample efficiency and generalization in robot learning, validated on simulated and real robots.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Articulated-Body Dynamics Network (ABD-Net) 的新型图神经网络架构，该架构基于前向动力学，旨在提高机器人学习中的样本效率和泛化能力，并在模拟和真实机器人上进行了验证。
summary_zh: 强化学习的最新研究表明，将连杆连接等铰接机器人的结构先验知识融入策略网络，可以提高学习效率。然而，尽管动力学性质在决定力和运动如何在身体中传播方面起着根本作用，但作为策略学习的归纳偏置，它在很大程度上仍未被探索。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了铰接体动力学网络（ABD-Net），这是一种基于正向动力学计算结构的新型图神经网络架构。具体而言，我们采用了铰接体算法中的惯性传播机制，以树状结构系统地将惯性量从子连杆聚合到父连杆，同时用可学习的参数取代物理量。将ABD-NET嵌入到策略行动器中，可以实现动力学感知的表示，捕捉动作如何在身体中传播，从而实现高效且稳健的策略学习。通过对模拟的人形、四足和单足跳跃机器人进行的实验，我们的方法与基于Transformer和GNN的基线相比，展示了更高的样本效率和对动力学变化的泛化能力。我们进一步验证了在真实的宇树G1和Go2机器人（先进的人形和四足平台）上学习到的策略，通过实时的sim-to-real迁移生成了动态、通用和稳健的运动行为。

title: ATG-MoE: Autoregressive trajectory generation with mixture-of-experts for assembly skill learning
summary: Flexible manufacturing requires robot systems that can adapt to constantly changing tasks, objects, and environments. However, traditional robot programming is labor-intensive and inflexible, while existing learning-based assembly methods often suffer from weak positional generalization, complex multi-stage designs, and limited multi-skill integration capability. To address these issues, this paper proposes ATG-MoE, an end-to-end autoregressive trajectory generation method with mixture of experts for assembly skill learning from demonstration. The proposed method establishes a closed-loop mapping from multi-modal inputs, including RGB-D observations, natural language instructions, and robot proprioception to manipulation trajectories. It integrates multi-modal feature fusion for scene and task understanding, autoregressive sequence modeling for temporally coherent trajectory generation, and a mixture-of-experts architecture for unified multi-skill learning. In contrast to conventional methods that separate visual perception and control or train different skills independently, ATG-MoE directly incorporates visual information into trajectory generation and supports efficient multi-skill integration within a single model. We train and evaluate the proposed method on eight representative assembly skills from a pressure-reducing valve assembly task. Experimental results show that ATG-MoE achieves strong overall performance in simulation, with an average grasp success rate of 96.3% and an average overall success rate of 91.8%, while also demonstrating strong generalization and effective multi-skill integration. Real-world experiments further verify its practicality for multi-skill industrial assembly. The project page can be found at https://hwh23.github.io/ATG-MoE
tldr: The paper introduces ATG-MoE, an autoregressive trajectory generation method using a mixture-of-experts architecture for learning diverse assembly skills from demonstration, achieving high success rates in simulation and demonstrating real-world practicality. It integrates multi-modal inputs and supports efficient multi-skill learning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ATG-MoE，一种使用混合专家架构的自回归轨迹生成方法，用于学习各种演示装配技能，在仿真中实现了很高的成功率，并展示了现实世界的实用性。它集成了多模态输入，并支持高效的多技能学习。
summary_zh: 柔性制造要求机器人系统能够适应不断变化的作业任务、对象和环境。然而，传统的机器人编程劳动密集且缺乏灵活性，而现有的基于学习的装配方法通常存在位置泛化能力弱、多阶段设计复杂以及多技能集成能力有限等问题。为解决这些难题，本文提出了一种名为 ATG-MoE 的端到端自回归轨迹生成方法，该方法采用混合专家模型，用于从示教中学习装配技能。所提出的方法建立了一个从多模态输入（包括 RGB-D 观测、自然语言指令和机器人自身感知信息）到操作轨迹的闭环映射。它集成了用于场景和任务理解的多模态特征融合、用于生成时间连贯轨迹的自回归序列建模以及用于统一多技能学习的混合专家架构。与将视觉感知和控制分离或独立训练不同技能的传统方法相比，ATG-MoE 直接将视觉信息融入到轨迹生成中，并在单个模型中支持高效的多技能集成。我们对一种减压阀装配任务中的八种代表性装配技能进行了训练和评估。实验结果表明，ATG-MoE 在仿真中实现了强大的整体性能，平均抓取成功率为 96.3%，平均整体成功率为 91.8%，同时还表现出强大的泛化能力和有效的多技能集成。现实世界实验进一步验证了其在多技能工业装配中的实用性。项目主页地址：https://hwh23.github.io/ATG-MoE

title: MERGE: Guided Vision-Language Models for Multi-Actor Event Reasoning and Grounding in Human-Robot Interaction
summary: We introduce MERGE, a system for situational grounding of actors, objects, and events in dynamic human-robot group interactions. Effective collaboration in such settings requires consistent situational awareness, built on persistent representations of people and objects and an episodic abstraction of events. MERGE achieves this by uniquely identifying physical instances of actors (humans or robots) and objects and structuring them into actor-action-object relations, ensuring temporal consistency across interactions. Central to MERGE is the integration of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) guided with a perception pipeline: a lightweight streaming module continuously processes visual input to detect changes and selectively invokes the VLM only when necessary. This decoupled design preserves the reasoning power and zero-shot generalization of VLMs while improving efficiency, avoiding both the high monetary cost and the latency of frame-by-frame captioning that leads to fragmented and delayed outputs. To address the absence of suitable benchmarks for multi-actor collaboration, we introduce the GROUND dataset, which offers fine-grained situational annotations of multi-person and human-robot interactions. On this dataset, our approach improves the average grounding score by a factor of 2 compared to the performance of VLM-only baselines - including GPT-4o, GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Flash - while also reducing run-time by a factor of 4. The code and data are available at www.github.com/HRI-EU/merge.
tldr: The paper introduces MERGE, a system that integrates Vision-Language Models with a perception pipeline for improved situational grounding in dynamic human-robot group interactions, outperforming VLM-only baselines on a new dataset, GROUND, with higher accuracy and efficiency.
tldr_zh: 这篇论文介绍了MERGE，该系统将视觉-语言模型与感知管道集成，以改进动态人机协作中的情境理解。 在新的数据集GROUND上，MERGE优于仅使用视觉-语言模型的基线方法，具有更高的准确性和效率。
summary_zh: 我们提出了MERGE，一个用于动态人-机器人群体交互中对行动者、物体和事件进行情境定位的系统。在这种环境中，有效的协作需要一致的情境感知，这建立在对人和物体的持久表示以及对事件的情节抽象之上。MERGE通过唯一标识行动者（人或机器人）和物体的物理实例，并将它们组织成行动者-动作-物体关系来实现这一点，从而确保跨交互的时间一致性。MERGE的核心是集成了一个由感知流水线引导的视觉-语言模型（VLM）：一个轻量级的流媒体模块持续处理视觉输入以检测变化，并在必要时选择性地调用VLM。这种解耦设计保留了VLM的推理能力和零样本泛化能力，同时提高了效率，避免了逐帧描述所带来的高昂成本和延迟，后者会导致输出的碎片化和延迟。为了解决缺乏适用于多行动者协作的基准测试的问题，我们引入了GROUND数据集，该数据集提供了对多人和人-机器人交互的细粒度情境标注。在这个数据集上，与仅使用VLM的基线模型（包括GPT-4o、GPT-5和Gemini 2.5 Flash）的性能相比，我们的方法将平均定位得分提高了2倍，同时将运行时间减少了4倍。代码和数据可在 www.github.com/HRI-EU/merge 获取。

title: V-Dreamer: Automating Robotic Simulation and Trajectory Synthesis via Video Generation Priors
summary: Training generalist robots demands large-scale, diverse manipulation data, yet real-world collection is prohibitively expensive, and existing simulators are often constrained by fixed asset libraries and manual heuristics. To bridge this gap, we present V-Dreamer, a fully automated framework that generates open-vocabulary, simulation-ready manipulation environments and executable expert trajectories directly from natural language instructions. V-Dreamer employs a novel generative pipeline that constructs physically grounded 3D scenes using large language models and 3D generative models, validated by geometric constraints to ensure stable, collision-free layouts. Crucially, for behavior synthesis, we leverage video generation models as rich motion priors. These visual predictions are then mapped into executable robot trajectories via a robust Sim-to-Gen visual-kinematic alignment module utilizing CoTracker3 and VGGT. This pipeline supports high visual diversity and physical fidelity without manual intervention. To evaluate the generated data, we train imitation learning policies on synthesized trajectories encompassing diverse object and environment variations. Extensive evaluations on tabletop manipulation tasks using the Piper robotic arm demonstrate that our policies robustly generalize to unseen objects in simulation and achieve effective sim-to-real transfer, successfully manipulating novel real-world objects.
tldr: V-Dreamer automates the generation of robotic simulation environments and trajectories from language instructions using LLMs, 3D generative models, and video generation as motion priors for imitation learning and sim-to-real transfer.
tldr_zh: V-Dreamer 利用大型语言模型、3D生成模型和视频生成作为运动先验，自动从语言指令生成机器人仿真环境和轨迹，用于模仿学习和模拟到真实的迁移。
summary_zh: 训练通用机器人需要大规模、多样性的操作数据，但现实世界的数据采集成本过高，而现有的模拟器往往受限于固定的资产库和手动启发式方法。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了V-Dreamer，一个全自动框架，能够直接从自然语言指令生成具有开放词汇表、可用于仿真的操作环境和可执行的专家轨迹。V-Dreamer采用一种新颖的生成流水线，该流水线利用大型语言模型和3D生成模型构建物理上合理的3D场景，并通过几何约束进行验证，以确保稳定、无碰撞的布局。至关重要的是，在行为合成方面，我们利用视频生成模型作为丰富的运动先验。然后，通过一个稳健的Sim-to-Gen视觉-运动学对齐模块（利用CoTracker3和VGGT），将这些视觉预测映射为可执行的机器人轨迹。该流水线支持高度的视觉多样性和物理保真度，无需人工干预。为了评估生成的数据，我们训练了基于合成轨迹的模仿学习策略，涵盖了各种物体和环境变化。在使用Piper机械臂的桌面操作任务上的大量评估表明，我们的策略能够稳健地泛化到模拟环境中未见过的物体，并实现有效的模拟到真实世界的迁移，成功地操作了新的真实世界物体。

title: Empathetic Motion Generation for Humanoid Educational Robots via Reasoning-Guided Vision--Language--Motion Diffusion Architecture
summary: This article suggests a reasoning-guided vision-language-motion diffusion framework (RG-VLMD) for generating instruction-aware co-speech gestures for humanoid robots in educational scenarios. The system integrates multi-modal affective estimation, pedagogical reasoning, and teaching-act-conditioned motion synthesis to enable adaptive and semantically consistent robot behavior. A gated mixture-of-experts model predicts Valence/Arousal from input text, visual, and acoustic features, which then mapped to discrete teaching-act categories through an affect-driven policy.These signals condition a diffusion-based motion generator using clip-level intent and frame-level instructional schedules via additive latent restriction with auxiliary action-group supervision. Compared to a baseline diffusion model, our proposed method produces more structured and distinctive motion patterns, as verified by motion statics and pairwise distance analysis. Generated motion sequences remain physically plausible and can be retargeted to a NAO robot for real-time execution. The results reveal that reasoning-guided instructional conditioning improves gesture controllability and pedagogical expressiveness in educational human-robot interaction.
tldr: This paper introduces a reasoning-guided vision-language-motion diffusion framework (RG-VLMD) for generating empathetic co-speech gestures for educational humanoid robots, improving gesture controllability and pedagogical expressiveness.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种推理引导的视觉-语言-动作扩散框架 (RG-VLMD)，用于为教育人形机器人生成富有同理心的口语手势，从而提高手势的可控性和教学表现力。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种推理引导的视觉-语言-运动扩散框架（RG-VLMD），用于在教育场景中为类人机器人生成指令感知的伴随语音手势。该系统集成了多模态情感估计、教学推理和教学行为条件运动合成，以实现自适应且语义一致的机器人行为。一个门控混合专家模型从输入的文本、视觉和声学特征中预测效价/唤醒度，然后通过情感驱动策略将其映射到离散的教学行为类别。这些信号通过附加潜在限制（辅助动作组监督）并使用片段级别的意图和帧级别的教学计划来调节基于扩散的运动生成器。与基线扩散模型相比，我们的方法产生了更结构化和更具区分性的运动模式，这通过运动统计和成对距离分析得到了验证。生成的运动序列保持物理上的合理性，并且可以重新定向到NAO机器人以进行实时执行。结果表明，推理引导的教学条件反射提高了教育人机交互中的手势可控性和教学表现力。

title: REST: Receding Horizon Explorative Steiner Tree for Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation
summary: Zero-shot object-goal navigation (ZSON) requires navigating unknown environments to find a target object without task-specific training. Prior hierarchical training-free solutions invest in scene understanding (\textit{belief}) and high-level decision-making (\textit{policy}), yet overlook the design of \textit{option}, i.e., a subgoal candidate proposed from evolving belief and presented to policy for selection. In practice, options are reduced to isolated waypoints scored independently: single destinations hide the value gathered along the journey; an unstructured collection obscures the relationships among candidates. Our insight is that the option space should be a \textit{tree of paths}. Full paths expose en-route information gain that destination-only scoring systematically neglects; a tree of shared segments enables coarse-to-fine LLM reasoning that dismisses or pursues entire branches before examining individual leaves, compressing the combinatorial path space into an efficient hierarchy. We instantiate this insight in \textbf{REST} (Receding Horizon Explorative Steiner Tree), a training-free framework that (1) builds an explicit open-vocabulary 3D map from online RGB-D streams; (2) grows an agent-centric tree of safe and informative paths as the option space via sampling-based planning; and (3) textualizes each branch into a spatial narrative and selects the next-best path through chain-of-thought LLM reasoning. Across the Gibson, HM3D, and HSSD benchmarks, REST consistently ranks among the top methods in success rate while achieving the best or second-best path efficiency, demonstrating a favorable efficiency-success balance.
tldr: The paper introduces REST, a training-free framework for zero-shot object-goal navigation that leverages a receding horizon explorative Steiner tree to generate a tree of path options, which are then reasoned about by an LLM to select the next-best path.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为REST的免训练框架，用于零样本物体目标导航。该框架利用后退水平探索性斯坦纳树生成路径选项树，然后通过大型语言模型（LLM）对这些选项进行推理，从而选择最佳的下一个路径。
summary_zh: 零样本物体目标导航 (ZSON) 需要在未知环境中导航以找到目标物体，而无需进行特定任务的训练。先前的无训练分层解决方案侧重于场景理解（\textit{belief}）和高层决策（\textit{policy}），但忽略了\textit{option}的设计，即一种从演化belief中提出的子目标候选，并呈现给policy以供选择。在实践中，options 被简化为独立评分的孤立航路点：单个目的地隐藏了沿途收集的价值；非结构化的集合模糊了候选者之间的关系。我们的洞见是，option空间应该是一个\textit{路径树}。完整的路径暴露了仅目的地评分系统性忽略的途中的信息增益；共享片段的树能够进行从粗到精的大语言模型 (LLM) 推理，在检查单个叶节点之前驳回或追求整个分支，从而将组合路径空间压缩成一个高效的层次结构。我们将这一洞见在\textbf{REST} (Receding Horizon Explorative Steiner Tree，后退视界探索性斯坦纳树) 中实例化，这是一个无需训练的框架，它 (1) 从在线 RGB-D 流构建一个显式的开放词汇 3D 地图；(2) 通过基于采样的规划，构建一个以智能体为中心的、安全且信息丰富的路径树作为option空间；以及 (3) 将每个分支文本化为空间叙事，并通过思维链式 LLM 推理选择下一个最佳路径。在 Gibson、HM3D 和 HSSD 基准测试中，REST 在成功率方面始终名列前茅，同时实现了最佳或次佳的路径效率，展示了有利的效率-成功平衡。

title: Robotic Agentic Platform for Intelligent Electric Vehicle Disassembly
summary: Electric vehicles (EV) create an urgent need for scalable battery recycling, yet disassembly of EV battery packs remains largely manual due to high design variability. We present our Robotic Agentic Platform for Intelligent Disassembly (RAPID), designed to investigate perception-driven manipulation, flexible automation, and AI-assisted robot programming in realistic recycling scenarios. The system integrates a gantry-mounted industrial manipulator, RGB-D perception, and an automated nut-running tool for fastener removal on a full-scale EV battery pack. An open-vocabulary object detection pipeline achieves 0.9757 mAP50, enabling reliable identification of screws, nuts, busbars, and other components. We experimentally evaluate (n=204) three one-shot fastener removal strategies: taught-in poses (97% success rate, 24 min duration), one-shot vision execution (57%, 29 min), and visual servoing (83%, 36 min), comparing success rate and disassembly time for the battery's top cover fasteners. To support flexible interaction, we introduce agentic AI specifications for robotic disassembly tasks, allowing LLM agents to translate high-level instructions into robot actions through structured tool interfaces and ROS services. We evaluate SmolAgents with GPT-4o-mini and Qwen 3.5 9B/4B on edge hardware. Tool-based interfaces achieve 100% task completion, while automatic ROS service discovery shows 43.3% failure rates, highlighting the need for structured robot APIs for reliable LLM-driven control. This open-source platform enables systematic investigation of human-robot collaboration, agentic robot programming, and increasingly autonomous disassembly workflows, providing a practical foundation for research toward scalable robotic battery recycling.
tldr: The paper presents a robotic platform (RAPID) for intelligent disassembly of EV batteries, integrating perception, manipulation, and LLM-based agentic control, experimentally validating different disassembly strategies and highlighting the importance of structured robot APIs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个用于智能拆卸电动汽车电池的机器人平台 (RAPID)，集成了感知、操作和基于 LLM 的智能体控制，通过实验验证了不同的拆卸策略，并强调了结构化机器人 API 的重要性。
summary_zh: 电动汽车（EV）引发了对可扩展电池回收的迫切需求，但由于高设计变异性，电动汽车电池组的拆解仍然主要依赖人工。我们提出了用于智能拆解的机器人代理平台（RAPID），旨在研究在实际回收场景中的感知驱动操作、柔性自动化和人工智能辅助机器人编程。该系统集成了一个龙门式工业机械臂、RGB-D 感知和一个自动拧紧工具，用于在全尺寸电动汽车电池组上移除紧固件。一个开放词汇的目标检测流程达到了 0.9757 mAP50，能够可靠地识别螺钉、螺母、汇流条和其他组件。我们通过实验评估（n=204）了三种一次性紧固件移除策略：示教姿态（97% 成功率，24 分钟时长）、一次性视觉执行（57%，29 分钟）和视觉伺服（83%，36 分钟），比较了电池顶盖紧固件的成功率和拆解时间。为了支持灵活的交互，我们引入了用于机器人拆解任务的代理式人工智能规范，允许 LLM 代理通过结构化的工具接口和 ROS 服务将高级指令转换为机器人动作。我们使用 GPT-4o-mini 和 Qwen 3.5 9B/4B 在边缘硬件上评估了 SmolAgents。基于工具的接口实现了 100% 的任务完成率，而自动 ROS 服务发现显示出 43.3% 的失败率，突出了对结构化机器人 API 以实现可靠的 LLM 驱动控制的需求。这个开源平台能够系统地研究人机协作、代理式机器人编程和日益自主的拆解工作流程，为可扩展的机器人电池回收研究提供了一个实践基础。

title: MemoAct: Atkinson-Shiffrin-Inspired Memory-Augmented Visuomotor Policy for Robotic Manipulation
summary: Memory-augmented robotic policies are essential in handling memory-dependent tasks. However, existing approaches typically rely on simple observation window extensions, struggling to simultaneously achieve precise task state tracking and robust long-horizon retention. To overcome these challenges, inspired by the Atkinson-Shiffrin memory model, we propose MemoAct, a hierarchical memory-based policy that leverages distinct memory tiers to tackle specific bottlenecks. Specifically, lossless short-term memory ensures precise task state tracking, while compressed long-term memory enables robust long-horizon retention. To enrich the evaluation landscape, we construct MemoryRTBench based on RoboTwin 2.0, specifically tailored to assess policy capabilities in task state tracking and long-horizon retention. Extensive experiments across simulated and real-world scenarios demonstrate that MemoAct achieves superior performance compared to both existing Markovian baselines and history-aware policies. The project page is \href{https://tlf-tlf.github.io/MemoActPage/}{available}.
tldr: The paper introduces MemoAct, a memory-augmented visuomotor policy for robotic manipulation inspired by the Atkinson-Shiffrin model, utilizing distinct memory tiers for precise task state tracking and robust long-horizon retention, and evaluated on a new benchmark MemoryRTBench.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MemoAct，一种受Atkinson-Shiffrin模型启发的记忆增强型机器人视觉运动策略，它利用不同的记忆层来实现精确的任务状态跟踪和强大的长时保持能力，并在新的基准MemoryRTBench上进行了评估。
summary_zh: 记忆增强型机器人策略对于处理依赖于记忆的任务至关重要。然而，现有方法通常依赖于简单的观察窗口扩展，难以同时实现精确的任务状态跟踪和鲁棒的长程记忆保持。为了克服这些挑战，我们受到Atkinson-Shiffrin记忆模型的启发，提出了一种名为MemoAct的分层记忆策略，该策略利用不同的记忆层来解决特定的瓶颈。具体而言，无损短期记忆确保了精确的任务状态跟踪，而压缩的长期记忆则实现了鲁棒的长程记忆保持。为了丰富评估维度，我们在RoboTwin 2.0的基础上构建了MemoryRTBench，专门用于评估策略在任务状态跟踪和长程记忆保持方面的能力。在模拟和真实世界场景下进行的大量实验表明，与现有的马尔可夫基线和历史感知策略相比，MemoAct取得了更优越的性能。项目主页见\href{https://tlf-tlf.github.io/MemoActPage/}{available}。

title: Efficient and Versatile Quadrupedal Skating: Optimal Co-design via Reinforcement Learning and Bayesian Optimization
summary: In this paper, we present a hardware-control co-design approach that enables efficient and versatile roller skating on quadrupedal robots equipped with passive wheels. Passive-wheel skating reduces leg inertia and improves energy efficiency, particularly at high speeds. However, the absence of direct wheel actuation tightly couples mechanical design and control. To unlock the full potential of this modality, we formulate a bilevel optimization framework: an upper-level Bayesian Optimization searches the mechanical design space, while a lower-level Reinforcement Learning trains a motor control policy for each candidate design. The resulting design-policy pairs not only outperform human-engineered baselines, but also exhibit versatile behaviors such as hockey stop (rapid braking by turning sideways to maximize friction) and self-aligning motion (automatic reorientation to improve energy efficiency in the direction of travel), offering the first system-level study of dynamic skating motion on quadrupedal robots.
tldr: This paper presents a co-design approach using Bayesian Optimization and Reinforcement Learning to develop efficient and versatile quadrupedal robots that can roller skate, achieving superior performance and novel behaviors compared to human-engineered designs.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种通过贝叶斯优化和强化学习的协同设计方法，开发出高效且通用的四足机器人滑冰方案，与人工设计的方案相比，实现了卓越的性能和 novel 的行为表现。
summary_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种硬件-控制协同设计方法，该方法使得配备被动轮的四足机器人能够高效且灵活地进行轮滑运动。被动轮滑运动降低了腿部惯性，并提高了能量效率，尤其是在高速运动时。然而，缺乏直接的车轮驱动将机械设计与控制紧密耦合。为了充分挖掘这种模式的潜力，我们提出了一个双层优化框架：上层贝叶斯优化搜索机械设计空间，而下层强化学习为每个候选设计训练一个电机控制策略。由此产生的设计-策略对不仅优于人工设计的基线，而且还表现出多种运动行为，例如曲棍球式停止（通过横向转向以最大化摩擦力来进行快速制动）和自对准运动（自动重新定向以提高沿运动方向的能量效率），从而提供了对四足机器人动态轮滑运动的首次系统级研究。

title: Shifting Uncertainty to Critical Moments: Towards Reliable Uncertainty Quantification for VLA Model
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable general-purpose robotic policies by mapping visual observations and language instructions to low-level actions, but they often lack reliable introspection. A common practice is to compute a token-level uncertainty signal and take its mean over a rollout. However, mean aggregation can dilute short-lived but safety-critical uncertainty spikes in continuous control. In particular, successful rollouts may contain localized high-entropy segments due to benign noise or non-critical micro-adjustments, while failure rollouts can appear low-entropy for most timesteps and only exhibit brief spikes near the onset of failure. We propose a unified uncertainty quantification approach for predicting rollout success versus failure that (1) uses max-based sliding window pooling to preserve transient risk signals, (2) applies motion-aware stability weighting to emphasize high-frequency action oscillations associated with unstable behaviors, and (3) performs DoF-adaptive calibration via Bayesian Optimization to prioritize kinematically critical axes. Experiments on the LIBERO benchmark show that our method substantially improves failure prediction accuracy and yields more reliable signals for failure detection, which can support downstream human-in-the-loop interventions.
tldr: This paper introduces a new uncertainty quantification method for Vision-Language-Action models that uses max-based pooling, motion-aware weighting, and DoF-adaptive calibration to improve failure prediction accuracy and enable human intervention.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新的视觉-语言-动作模型的不确定性量化方法，该方法使用基于最大值的池化、运动感知加权和DoF自适应校准，以提高故障预测的准确性并支持人工干预。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型能够通过将视觉观测和语言指令映射到低级动作来实现通用机器人策略，但它们通常缺乏可靠的自省能力。一种常见的做法是计算令牌级别的置信度不确定性信号，并对其在整个运行过程中取平均值。然而，在连续控制中，平均聚合可能会稀释短暂但对安全至关重要的不确定性峰值。特别是，成功的运行过程可能包含由于良性噪声或非关键微调引起的局部高熵片段，而失败的运行过程在大多数时间步长中可能显得低熵，并且仅在失败开始时表现出短暂的峰值。我们提出了一种统一的不确定性量化方法，用于预测运行过程的成功与失败，该方法 (1) 使用基于最大值的滑动窗口池化来保留瞬时风险信号，(2) 应用运动感知的稳定性加权来强调与不稳定行为相关的高频动作振荡，以及 (3) 通过贝叶斯优化执行自由度自适应校准，以优先考虑运动学上关键的轴。在 LIBERO 基准测试上的实验表明，我们的方法显著提高了失败预测的准确性，并产生了更可靠的失败检测信号，从而可以支持下游的人在环干预。

title: ManiDreams: An Open-Source Library for Robust Object Manipulation via Uncertainty-aware Task-specific Intuitive Physics
summary: Dynamics models, whether simulators or learned world models, have long been central to robotic manipulation, but most focus on minimizing prediction error rather than confronting a more fundamental challenge: real-world manipulation is inherently uncertain. We argue that robust manipulation under uncertainty is fundamentally an integration problem: uncertainties must be represented, propagated, and constrained within the planning loop, not merely suppressed during training. We present and open-source ManiDreams, a modular framework for uncertainty-aware manipulation planning over intuitive physics models. It realizes this integration through composable abstractions for distributional state representation, backend-agnostic dynamics prediction, and declarative constraint specification for action optimization. The framework explicitly addresses three sources of uncertainty: perceptual, parametric, and structural. It wraps any base policy with a sample-predict-constrain loop that evaluates candidate actions against distributional outcomes, adding robustness without retraining. Experiments on ManiSkill tasks show that ManiDreams maintains robust performance under various perturbations where the RL baseline degrades significantly. Runnable examples on pushing, picking, catching, and real-world deployment demonstrate flexibility across different policies, optimizers, physics backends, and executors. The framework is publicly available at https://github.com/Rice-RobotPI-Lab/ManiDreams
tldr: ManiDreams is an open-source framework for robust robot manipulation planning that handles uncertainty by integrating distributional state representation, dynamics prediction, and declarative constraint specification. It shows improved robustness in manipulation tasks compared to RL baselines.
tldr_zh: ManiDreams是一个开源框架，用于鲁棒的机器人操作规划，它通过整合分布式的状态表示、动力学预测和声明式的约束规范来处理不确定性。它在操作任务中表现出比强化学习基线更好的鲁棒性。
summary_zh: 动力学模型，无论是模拟器还是学习到的世界模型，长期以来一直是机器人操作的核心，但大多数都侧重于最小化预测误差，而不是应对一个更根本的挑战：现实世界的操作本质上是不确定的。我们认为，在不确定性下进行鲁棒操作本质上是一个整合问题：不确定性必须在规划循环中进行表示、传播和约束，而不仅仅是在训练中被抑制。我们提出并开源了ManiDreams，这是一个模块化的框架，用于基于直观物理模型进行不确定性感知的操作规划。它通过可组合的抽象概念来实现这种整合，这些抽象概念包括：分布式的状态表示、与后端无关的动力学预测以及用于动作优化的声明式约束规范。该框架明确地解决了三种不确定性来源：感知上的、参数上的和结构上的。它使用一个样本-预测-约束循环来包装任何基本策略，该循环根据分布式的输出评估候选动作，从而在无需重新训练的情况下增加鲁棒性。在ManiSkill任务上的实验表明，在各种扰动下，ManiDreams能够保持鲁棒的性能，而强化学习基线的性能则会显著下降。关于推、抓、接的以及现实世界部署的可运行示例展示了在不同策略、优化器、物理后端和执行器上的灵活性。该框架已在https://github.com/Rice-RobotPI-Lab/ManiDreams 上公开。

title: SG-CoT: An Ambiguity-Aware Robotic Planning Framework using Scene Graph Representations
summary: Ambiguity poses a major challenge to large language models (LLMs) used as robotic planners. In this letter, we present Scene Graph-Chain-of-Thought (SG-CoT), a two-stage framework where LLMs iteratively query a scene graph representation of the environment to detect and clarify ambiguities. First, a structured scene graph representation of the environment is constructed from input observations, capturing objects, their attributes, and relationships with other objects. Second, the LLM is equipped with retrieval functions to query portions of the scene graph that are relevant to the provided instruction. This grounds the reasoning process of the LLM in the observation, increasing the reliability of robotic planners under ambiguous situations. SG-CoT also allows the LLM to identify the source of ambiguity and pose a relevant disambiguation question to the user or another robot. Extensive experimentation demonstrates that SG-CoT consistently outperforms prior methods, with a minimum of 10% improvement in question accuracy and a minimum success rate increase of 4% in single-agent and 15% in multi-agent environments, validating its effectiveness for more generalizable robot planning.
tldr: The paper introduces SG-CoT, a framework employing scene graphs and LLMs for improved robotic planning by addressing ambiguities through iterative querying and disambiguation questions. It demonstrates significant performance gains in single and multi-agent environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SG-CoT，一个利用场景图和大型语言模型的框架，通过迭代查询和消除歧义问题来改进机器人规划。它展示了在单智能体和多智能体环境中显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 歧义性给用作机器人规划器的大型语言模型（LLMs）带来了重大挑战。在本文中，我们提出了一种双阶段框架——场景图-思维链（SG-CoT），其中LLMs迭代查询环境的场景图表示，以检测和澄清歧义。首先，从输入的观测中构建环境的结构化场景图表示，捕捉对象、它们的属性以及与其他对象的关系。其次，LLM配有检索功能，用于查询场景图中与所提供指令相关的部分。这使得LLM的推理过程基于观测，提高了机器人规划器在模糊情况下的可靠性。SG-CoT还允许LLM识别歧义的来源，并向用户或其他机器人提出相关的消除歧义问题。大量的实验表明，SG-CoT始终优于先前的方法，在问题准确率方面至少提高了10%，在单智能体环境中成功率至少提高了4%，在多智能体环境中成功率至少提高了15%，验证了其在更具泛化能力的机器人规划中的有效性。

title: Rapid Adaptation of Particle Dynamics for Generalized Deformable Object Mobile Manipulation
summary: We address the challenge of learning to manipulate deformable objects with unknown dynamics. In non-rigid objects, the dynamics parameters define how they react to interactions -- how they stretch, bend, compress, and move -- and they are critical to determining the optimal actions to perform a manipulation task successfully. In other robotic domains, such as legged locomotion and in-hand rigid object manipulation, state-of-the-art approaches can handle unknown dynamics using Rapid Motor Adaptation (RMA). Through a supervised procedure in simulation that encodes each rigid object's dynamics, such as mass and position, these approaches learn a policy that conditions actions on a vector of latent dynamic parameters inferred from sequences of state-actions. However, in deformable object manipulation, the object's dynamics not only includes its mass and position, but also how the shape of the object changes. Our key insight is that the recent ground-truth particle positions of a deformable object in simulation capture changes in the object's shape, making it possible to extend RMA to deformable object manipulation. This key insight allows us to develop RAPiD, a two-phase method that learns to perform real-robot deformable object mobile manipulation by: 1) learning a visuomotor policy conditioned on the object's dynamics embedding, which is encoded from the object's privileged information in simulation, such as its mass and ground-truth particle positions, and 2) learning to infer this embedding using non-privileged information instead, such as robot visual observations and actions, so that the learned policy can transfer to the real world. On a mobile manipulator with 22 degrees of freedom, RAPiD enables over 80%+ success rates across two vision-based deformable object mobile manipulation tasks in the real world, under various object dynamics, categories, and instances.
tldr: This paper introduces RAPiD, a method for rapidly adapting to unknown dynamics in deformable object manipulation by using particle positions to encode object dynamics, enabling real-world mobile manipulation with high success rates.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为RAPiD 的方法，通过使用粒子位置编码物体动力学，快速适应变形物体操作中未知的动力学，从而实现高成功率的真实世界移动操作。
summary_zh: 我们致力于解决学习操控具有未知动力学的可变形物体这一挑战。在非刚性物体中，动力学参数定义了它们对交互的反应——它们如何拉伸、弯曲、压缩和移动——并且它们对于确定成功执行操控任务的最佳动作至关重要。在其他机器人领域，例如腿式运动和手内刚性物体操控，最先进的方法可以使用快速运动自适应（RMA）来处理未知的动力学。通过在模拟中进行的监督过程，该过程编码了每个刚性物体的动力学，例如质量和位置，这些方法学习一种策略，该策略基于从状态-动作序列推断出的潜在动力学参数向量来调节动作。然而，在可变形物体操控中，物体的动力学不仅包括其质量和位置，还包括物体的形状如何变化。我们的关键洞察是，模拟中最近的可变形物体的真实粒子位置能够捕捉物体形状的变化，从而可以将RMA扩展到可变形物体操控。这一关键洞察使我们能够开发RAPiD，这是一种两阶段方法，该方法通过以下方式学习执行真实机器人可变形物体移动操控：1) 学习一种以物体动力学嵌入为条件的视觉运动策略，该嵌入编码自模拟中物体的特权信息，例如其质量和真实粒子位置；2) 学习使用非特权信息（例如机器人视觉观察和动作）推断此嵌入，以便学习到的策略可以转移到现实世界。在一个具有22个自由度的移动机械臂上，RAPiD能够在真实世界的各种物体动力学、类别和实例下，在两个基于视觉的可变形物体移动操控任务中实现超过80%的成功率。

title: GoalVLM: VLM-driven Object Goal Navigation for Multi-Agent System
summary:
Object-goal navigation has traditionally been limited to ground robots with closed-set object vocabularies. Existing multi-agent approaches depend on precomputed probabilistic graphs tied to fixed category sets, precluding generalization to novel goals at test time.
  We present GoalVLM, a cooperative multi-agent framework for zero-shot, open-vocabulary object navigation. GoalVLM integrates a Vision-Language Model (VLM) directly into the decision loop, SAM3 for text-prompted detection and segmentation, and SpaceOM for spatial reasoning, enabling agents to interpret free-form language goals and score frontiers via zero-shot semantic priors without retraining. Each agent builds a BEV semantic map from depth-projected voxel splatting, while a Goal Projector back-projects detections through calibrated depth into the map for reliable goal localization. A constraint-guided reasoning layer evaluates frontiers through a structured prompt chain (scene captioning, room-type classification, perception gating, multi-frontier ranking), injecting commonsense priors into exploration.
  We evaluate GoalVLM on GOAT-Bench val_unseen (360 multi-subtask episodes, 1032 sequential object-goal subtasks, HM3D scenes), where each episode requires navigating to a chain of 5-7 open-vocabulary targets. GoalVLM with N=2 agents achieves 55.8% subtask SR and 18.3% SPL, competitive with state-of-the-art methods while requiring no task-specific training. Ablation studies confirm the contributions of VLM-guided frontier reasoning and depth-projected goal localization.
tldr: The paper introduces GoalVLM, a multi-agent framework that uses a VLM to enable zero-shot, open-vocabulary object navigation in complex, chained tasks, achieving competitive performance without task-specific training.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 GoalVLM，一个多智能体框架。该框架利用视觉-语言模型实现零样本、开放词汇的目标导航，适用于复杂的链式任务，并在不需要特定任务训练的情况下取得了有竞争力的性能。
summary_zh:
目标导向导航传统上仅限于具有封闭集对象词汇表的地面机器人。现有的多智能体方法依赖于与固定类别集绑定的预计算概率图，从而排除了在测试时推广到新目标的能力。

我们提出了GoalVLM，一个用于零样本、开放词汇表目标导航的协作多智能体框架。GoalVLM将视觉语言模型(VLM)直接集成到决策循环中，使用SAM3进行文本提示驱动的检测和分割，以及SpaceOM进行空间推理，使智能体能够解释自由形式的语言目标，并通过零样本语义先验来对前沿进行评分，而无需重新训练。每个智能体通过深度投影体素溅射构建鸟瞰图(BEV)语义地图，同时目标投影器将检测结果通过校准后的深度反投影到地图中，以实现可靠的目标定位。一个约束引导的推理层通过结构化的提示链（场景描述、房间类型分类、感知门控、多前沿排序）来评估前沿，将常识先验注入到探索中。

我们在GOAT-Bench val_unseen上评估了GoalVLM (360个多子任务episode，1032个连续目标导向的子任务, HM3D场景), 其中每个episode要求导航到包含5-7个开放词汇表目标的链条。GoalVLM 在N=2个智能体的情况下实现了55.8%的子任务成功率(SR)和18.3%的SPL，可与最先进的方法相媲美，同时不需要特定于任务的训练。消融研究证实了VLM引导的前沿推理和深度投影目标定位的贡献。

title: R2-Dreamer: Redundancy-Reduced World Models without Decoders or Augmentation
summary: A central challenge in image-based Model-Based Reinforcement Learning (MBRL) is to learn representations that distill essential information from irrelevant visual details. While promising, reconstruction-based methods often waste capacity on large task-irrelevant regions. Decoder-free methods instead learn robust representations by leveraging Data Augmentation (DA), but reliance on such external regularizers limits versatility. We propose R2-Dreamer, a decoder-free MBRL framework with a self-supervised objective that serves as an internal regularizer, preventing representation collapse without resorting to DA. The core of our method is a redundancy-reduction objective inspired by Barlow Twins, which can be easily integrated into existing frameworks. On DeepMind Control Suite and Meta-World, R2-Dreamer is competitive with strong baselines such as DreamerV3 and TD-MPC2 while training 1.59x faster than DreamerV3, and yields substantial gains on DMC-Subtle with tiny task-relevant objects. These results suggest that an effective internal regularizer can enable versatile, high-performance decoder-free MBRL. Code is available at https://github.com/NM512/r2dreamer.
tldr: R2-Dreamer proposes a decoder-free MBRL framework using a self-supervised redundancy-reduction objective, inspired by Barlow Twins, to prevent representation collapse without data augmentation, achieving competitive results and faster training.
tldr_zh: R2-Dreamer 提出了一种无解码器的基于模型的强化学习框架，它使用一种受 Barlow Twins 启发的自监督冗余减少目标，以防止表征崩溃且无需数据增强，从而实现了有竞争力的结果和更快的训练速度。
summary_zh: 基于图像的基于模型的强化学习（MBRL）的一个核心挑战是学习能够从无关视觉细节中提取必要信息的表征。尽管基于重构的方法前景广阔，但通常会将大量能力浪费在与任务无关的大区域上。无解码器方法则通过利用数据增强（DA）来学习稳健的表征，但对这种外部正则化器的依赖限制了通用性。我们提出了R2-Dreamer，这是一个无解码器的MBRL框架，它具有一个自监督目标，可作为内部正则化器，防止表征坍塌，而无需诉诸DA。我们方法的核心是由Barlow Twins启发的冗余减少目标，它可以很容易地集成到现有框架中。在DeepMind Control Suite和Meta-World上，R2-Dreamer与DreamerV3和TD-MPC2等强大的基线模型相比具有竞争力，同时训练速度比DreamerV3快1.59倍，并且在具有微小任务相关对象的DMC-Subtle上产生了显著的增益。这些结果表明，有效的内部正则化器可以实现通用且高性能的无解码器MBRL。代码可在https://github.com/NM512/r2dreamer获得。

title: SAVeS: Steering Safety Judgments in Vision-Language Models via Semantic Cues
summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world and embodied settings where safety decisions depend on visual context. However, it remains unclear which visual evidence drives these judgments. We study whether multimodal safety behavior in VLMs can be steered by simple semantic cues. We introduce a semantic steering framework that applies controlled textual, visual, and cognitive interventions without changing the underlying scene content. To evaluate these effects, we propose SAVeS, a benchmark for situational safety under semantic cues, together with an evaluation protocol that separates behavioral refusal, grounded safety reasoning, and false refusals. Experiments across multiple VLMs and an additional state-of-the-art benchmark show that safety decisions are highly sensitive to semantic cues, indicating reliance on learned visual-linguistic associations rather than grounded visual understanding. We further demonstrate that automated steering pipelines can exploit these mechanisms, highlighting a potential vulnerability in multimodal safety systems.
tldr: The paper introduces SAVeS, a benchmark to study how semantic cues can steer safety judgments in VLMs. The results show that VLMs are highly sensitive to these cues, revealing a potential vulnerability in multimodal safety systems.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SAVeS，一个用于研究语义线索如何引导视觉语言模型（VLM）中的安全判断的基准。结果表明，VLM对这些线索非常敏感，揭示了多模态安全系统中的潜在漏洞。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）越来越多地部署在实际和具身环境中，在这些环境中，安全决策取决于视觉上下文。然而，目前尚不清楚哪些视觉证据驱动这些判断。我们研究了在VLMs中，多模态安全行为是否能被简单的语义线索所引导。我们引入了一个语义引导框架，它可以在不改变底层场景内容的情况下，应用受控的文本、视觉和认知干预。为了评估这些影响，我们提出了SAVeS，一个用于语义线索下情境安全的基准，以及一个评估协议，该协议区分了行为拒绝、基于事实的安全推理和错误拒绝。在多个VLMs和一个额外的最先进基准上进行的实验表明，安全决策对语义线索高度敏感，表明其依赖于已学习的视觉-语言关联，而不是基于事实的视觉理解。我们进一步证明，自动引导流程可以利用这些机制，突出了多模态安全系统中的一个潜在漏洞。

title: Act While Thinking: Accelerating LLM Agents via Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution
summary: LLM-powered agents are emerging as a dominant paradigm for autonomous task solving. Unlike standard inference workloads, agents operate in a strictly serial "LLM-tool" loop, where the LLM must wait for external tool execution at every step. This execution model introduces severe latency bottlenecks. To address this problem, we propose PASTE, a Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution method designed to hide tool latency through speculation. PASTE is based on the insight that although agent requests are semantically diverse, they exhibit stable application level control flows (recurring tool-call sequences) and predictable data dependencies (parameter passing between tools). By exploiting these properties, PASTE improves agent serving performance through speculative tool execution. Experimental results against state of the art baselines show that PASTE reduces average task completion time by 48.5% and improves tool execution throughput by 1.8x.
tldr: The paper introduces PASTE, a method to reduce latency in LLM agents by speculatively executing tool calls based on observed patterns in tool call sequences and data dependencies, achieving significant performance improvements.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了PASTE，一种通过基于观察到的工具调用序列和数据依赖中的模式推测性地执行工具调用来减少LLM代理中的延迟的方法，从而显著提高了性能。
summary_zh: 由大型语言模型驱动的智能体正逐渐成为自主解决任务的主流范式。与标准的推理工作负载不同，智能体以严格串行的“LLM-工具”循环运行，其中大型语言模型必须在每一步等待外部工具执行。这种执行模型会引入严重的延迟瓶颈。为了解决这个问题，我们提出 PASTE，一种模式感知推测性工具执行方法，旨在通过推测来隐藏工具延迟。 PASTE 基于这样一种洞察：尽管智能体请求在语义上是多样的，但它们表现出稳定的应用级控制流（重复出现的工具调用序列）和可预测的数据依赖关系（工具之间的参数传递）。通过利用这些特性，PASTE 通过推测性工具执行提高了智能体Serving性能。与最先进的基线相比，实验结果表明，PASTE 将平均任务完成时间缩短了 48.5%，并将工具执行吞吐量提高了 1.8 倍。

title: MultihopSpatial: Multi-hop Compositional Spatial Reasoning Benchmark for Vision-Language Model
summary: Spatial reasoning is foundational for Vision-Language Models (VLMs), particularly when deployed as Vision-Language-Action (VLA) agents in physical environments. However, existing benchmarks predominantly focus on elementary, single-hop relations, neglecting the multi-hop compositional reasoning and precise visual grounding essential for real-world scenarios. To address this, we introduce MultihopSpatial, offering three key contributions: (1) A comprehensive benchmark designed for multi-hop and compositional spatial reasoning, featuring 1- to 3-hop complex queries across diverse spatial perspectives. (2) Acc@50IoU, a complementary metric that simultaneously evaluates reasoning and visual grounding by requiring both answer selection and precise bounding box prediction - capabilities vital for robust VLA deployment. (3) MultihopSpatial-Train, a dedicated large-scale training corpus to foster spatial intelligence. Extensive evaluation of 37 state-of-the-art VLMs yields eight key insights, revealing that compositional spatial reasoning remains a formidable challenge. Finally, we demonstrate that reinforcement learning post-training on our corpus enhances both intrinsic VLM spatial reasoning and downstream embodied manipulation performance.
tldr: The paper introduces MultihopSpatial, a new benchmark and training dataset for multi-hop compositional spatial reasoning in VLMs, demonstrating that current VLMs struggle with such reasoning and that reinforcement learning finetuning on their dataset improves spatial reasoning and embodied manipulation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MultihopSpatial，一个新的用于视觉语言模型中多跳组合空间推理的基准测试和训练数据集。研究表明，当前的视觉语言模型在处理此类推理时表现不佳，而通过在他们的数据集上进行强化学习微调可以提高空间推理和具身操作能力。
summary_zh: 空间推理是视觉-语言模型（VLM）的基础，特别是当它们作为视觉-语言-动作（VLA）智能体部署在物理环境中时。然而，现有的基准测试主要集中在基本的、单跳关系上，忽略了真实世界场景中至关重要的多跳组合推理和精确的视觉定位。为了解决这个问题，我们推出了MultihopSpatial，提供了三个关键贡献：（1）一个为多跳和组合空间推理设计的综合基准测试，包含跨越不同空间视角的1到3跳复杂查询。（2）Acc@50IoU，一个补充性指标，通过同时要求答案选择和精确的边界框预测来评估推理和视觉定位——这两种能力对于稳健的VLA部署至关重要。（3）MultihopSpatial-Train，一个专门的大规模训练语料库，旨在培养空间智能。对37个最先进的VLM进行的广泛评估产生了八个关键见解，揭示了组合空间推理仍然是一个严峻的挑战。最后，我们证明了在我们的语料库上进行的强化学习后训练可增强固有的VLM空间推理能力和下游的具身操作性能。

title: Bridging Network Fragmentation: A Semantic-Augmented DRL Framework for UAV-aided VANETs
summary: Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANETs) are the digital cornerstone of autonomous driving, yet they suffer from severe network fragmentation in urban environments due to physical obstructions. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), with their high mobility, have emerged as a vital solution to bridge these connectivity gaps. However, traditional Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based UAV deployment strategies lack semantic understanding of road topology, often resulting in blind exploration and sample inefficiency. By contrast, Large Language Models (LLMs) possess powerful reasoning capabilities capable of identifying topological importance, though applying them to control tasks remains challenging. To address this, we propose the Semantic-Augmented DRL (SA-DRL) framework. Firstly, we propose a fragmentation quantification method based on Road Topology Graphs (RTG) and Dual Connected Graphs (DCG). Subsequently, we design a four-stage pipeline to transform a general-purpose LLM into a domain-specific topology expert. Finally, we propose the Semantic-Augmented PPO (SA-PPO) algorithm, which employs a Logit Fusion mechanism to inject the LLM's semantic reasoning directly into the policy as a prior, effectively guiding the agent toward critical intersections. Extensive high-fidelity simulations demonstrate that SA-PPO achieves state-of-the-art performance with remarkable efficiency, reaching baseline performance levels using only 26.6% of the training episodes. Ultimately, SA-PPO improves two key connectivity metrics by 13.2% and 23.5% over competing methods, while reducing energy consumption to just 28.2% of the baseline.
tldr: This paper introduces a Semantic-Augmented DRL (SA-DRL) framework, leveraging LLMs to enhance UAV deployment in VANETs by incorporating semantic understanding of road topology, resulting in improved connectivity and energy efficiency.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种语义增强的DRL (SA-DRL)框架，利用LLM通过结合道路拓扑的语义理解来增强VANET中无人机的部署，从而提高连接性和能源效率。
summary_zh: 车辆自组织网络（VANETs）是自动驾驶的数字基石，但由于物理障碍，它们在城市环境中遭受严重的网络碎片化。无人机（UAVs）凭借其高移动性，已成为弥合这些连接性差距的关键解决方案。然而，传统的基于深度强化学习（DRL）的无人机部署策略缺乏对道路拓扑的语义理解，常常导致盲目探索和样本效率低下。相比之下，大型语言模型（LLMs）拥有强大的推理能力，能够识别拓扑重要性，但将其应用于控制任务仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了语义增强的DRL（SA-DRL）框架。首先，我们提出了一种基于道路拓扑图（RTG）和双连通图（DCG）的碎片化量化方法。随后，我们设计了一个四阶段流程，将通用LLM转化为领域特定的拓扑专家。最后，我们提出了语义增强的PPO（SA-PPO）算法，该算法采用对数几率融合机制，将LLM的语义推理直接作为先验注入到策略中，有效地引导智能体朝着关键路口移动。大量高保真仿真表明，SA-PPO实现了最先进的性能，并具有显著的效率，仅使用26.6%的训练轮次就达到了基线性能水平。最终，SA-PPO在两个关键连接性指标上分别比竞争方法提高了13.2%和23.5%，同时将能耗降低到基线的28.2%。

title: RewardFlow: Topology-Aware Reward Propagation on State Graphs for Agentic RL with Large Language Models
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) holds significant promise for enhancing the agentic reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) with external environments. However, the inherent sparsity of terminal rewards hinders fine-grained, state-level optimization. Although process reward modeling offers a promising alternative, training dedicated reward models often entails substantial computational costs and scaling difficulties. To address these challenges, we introduce RewardFlow, a lightweight method for estimating state-level rewards tailored to agentic reasoning tasks. RewardFlow leverages the intrinsic topological structure of states within reasoning trajectories by constructing state graphs. This enables an analysis of state-wise contributions to success, followed by topology-aware graph propagation to quantify contributions and yield objective, state-level rewards. When integrated as dense rewards for RL optimization, RewardFlow substantially outperforms prior RL baselines across four agentic reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating superior performance, robustness, and training efficiency. The implementation of RewardFlow is publicly available at https://github.com/tmlr-group/RewardFlow.
tldr: RewardFlow is a lightweight method for estimating state-level rewards in agentic RL with LLMs by leveraging the topological structure of states, outperforming prior RL baselines in reasoning benchmarks.
tldr_zh: RewardFlow是一种轻量级方法，通过利用状态的拓扑结构来估计LLM智能体强化学习中的状态级奖励，在推理基准测试中优于先前的强化学习基线。
summary_zh: 强化学习（RL）为提升大型语言模型（LLM）与外部环境交互的代理推理能力展现出巨大潜力。然而，终端奖励固有的稀疏性阻碍了细粒度的、状态级别的优化。尽管过程奖励建模提供了一种有前景的替代方案，但训练专门的奖励模型通常需要大量的计算成本和扩展难度。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种轻量级方法 RewardFlow，用于估计代理推理任务中状态级别的奖励。RewardFlow通过构建状态图来利用推理轨迹中状态的内在拓扑结构。这使得我们能够分析各状态对成功的贡献，然后通过拓扑感知图传播来量化贡献并产生客观的状态级别奖励。当作为密集奖励集成到强化学习优化中时，RewardFlow在四个代理推理基准测试中显著优于先前的强化学习基线，展示了卓越的性能、鲁棒性和训练效率。RewardFlow的实现已在https://github.com/tmlr-group/RewardFlow公开提供。

title: Motion-o: Trajectory-Grounded Video Reasoning
summary: Recent research has made substantial progress on video reasoning, with many models leveraging spatio-temporal evidence chains to strengthen their inference capabilities. At the same time, a growing set of datasets and benchmarks now provides structured annotations designed to support and evaluate such reasoning. However, little attention has been paid to reasoning about \emph{how} objects move between observations: no prior work has articulated the motion patterns by connecting successive observations, leaving trajectory understanding implicit and difficult to verify. We formalize this missing capability as Spatial-Temporal-Trajectory (STT) reasoning and introduce \textbf{Motion-o}, a motion-centric video understanding extension to visual language models that makes trajectories explicit and verifiable. To enable motion reasoning, we also introduce a trajectory-grounding dataset artifact that expands sparse keyframe supervision via augmentation to yield denser bounding box tracks and a stronger trajectory-level training signal. Finally, we introduce Motion Chain of Thought (MCoT), a structured reasoning pathway that makes object trajectories through discrete \texttt{<motion/>} tag summarizing per-object direction, speed, and scale (of velocity) change to explicitly connect grounded observations into trajectories. To train Motion-o, we design a reward function that compels the model to reason directly over visual evidence, all while requiring no architectural modifications. Empirical results demonstrate that Motion-o improves spatial-temporal grounding and trajectory prediction while remaining fully compatible with existing frameworks, establishing motion reasoning as a critical extension for evidence-based video understanding. Code is available at https://github.com/ostadabbas/Motion-o.
tldr: The paper introduces Motion-o, a video understanding extension to VLMs that explicitly models and reasons about object trajectories by grounding them in visual evidence and using a Motion Chain of Thought, improving spatial-temporal understanding.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了Motion-o，一种视频理解扩展，它通过将目标轨迹置于视觉证据中并使用运动思维链（Motion Chain of Thought）来显式地建模和推理目标轨迹，从而提高空间-时间理解能力。
