summary_zh: 近期研究在视频推理方面取得了显著进展，许多模型利用时空证据链来增强其推理能力。与此同时，越来越多的数据集和基准测试提供了结构化标注，旨在支持和评估此类推理。然而，很少有研究关注于对物体在观察之间\emph{如何}移动的推理：先前的工作都没有阐明通过连接连续观察来体现的运动模式，导致轨迹理解是隐式的且难以验证。我们将这种缺失的能力形式化为时空轨迹（STT）推理，并引入 \textbf{Motion-o}，这是一个以运动为中心的视频理解扩展，用于视觉语言模型，它使轨迹变得显式且可验证。为了实现运动推理，我们还引入了一种轨迹对齐数据集工件，该工件通过增强来扩展稀疏关键帧监督，从而产生更密集的边界框轨迹和更强的轨迹级训练信号。最后，我们引入运动思维链（MCoT），这是一种结构化推理路径，它通过离散的 \texttt{<motion/>} 标签总结每个物体的方向、速度和尺度（速度的变化），从而将对齐的观察结果显式地连接到轨迹中。为了训练 Motion-o，我们设计了一个奖励函数，迫使模型直接对视觉证据进行推理，同时不需要任何架构修改。经验结果表明，Motion-o 改进了时空对齐和轨迹预测，同时与现有框架完全保持兼容，从而确立了运动推理作为基于证据的视频理解的关键扩展。代码可在 https://github.com/ostadabbas/Motion-o 获取。

title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
summary: Multi-turn LLM agents are increasingly important for solving complex, interactive tasks, and reinforcement learning (RL) is a key ingredient for improving their long-horizon behavior. However, RL training requires generating large numbers of sandboxed rollout trajectories, and existing infrastructures often couple rollout orchestration with the training loop, making systems hard to migrate and maintain. Under the rollout-as-a-service philosophy, we present ProRL Agent , a scalable infrastructure that serves the full agentic rollout lifecycle through an API service. ProRL Agent also provides standardized and extensible sandbox environments that support diverse agentic tasks in rootless HPC settings. We validate ProRL Agent through RL training on software engineering, math, STEM, and coding tasks. ProRL Agent is open-sourced and integrated as part of NVIDIA NeMo Gym.
tldr: ProRL Agent provides a scalable, open-source infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents by offering rollout generation as a service, addressing infrastructure challenges in RL training for complex tasks.
tldr_zh: ProRL Agent 提供了一个可扩展的开源基础设施，用于多轮 LLM 智能体的 RL 训练，通过提供 rollout 生成服务，解决了复杂任务中 RL 训练的基础设施挑战。
summary_zh: 多轮LLM智能体在解决复杂的交互式任务中正变得日益重要，而强化学习（RL）是提升其长期行为的关键要素。然而，RL训练需要生成大量沙盒推演轨迹，并且现有的基础设施通常将推演编排与训练循环耦合在一起，导致系统难以迁移和维护。基于“推演即服务”的理念，我们提出了ProRL Agent，一个通过API服务提供完整的智能体推演生命周期的可扩展基础设施。ProRL Agent还提供了标准化且可扩展的沙盒环境，支持在无根HPC环境中执行各种智能体任务。我们通过在软件工程、数学、STEM和编码任务上进行的RL训练验证了ProRL Agent。ProRL Agent已开源并作为NVIDIA NeMo Gym的一部分集成。

title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation
summary: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) excel at semantic understanding but struggle with fine grained spatial grounding, as the model must implicitly infer complex geometry without ever producing a spatial interpretation. We present Perceptio, a perception enhanced LVLM with 2D and 3D spatial reasoning abilities, enabled via explicit semantic segmentation tokens and depth tokens generated directly within the autoregressive sequence. Concretely, we (i) distill a VQVAE depth codebook from a strong monocular teacher to tokenize dense depth into compact sequences, and (ii) integrate SAM2 based semantic segmentation tokens and VQ-VAE depth tokens inside the LLM so the model first emits spatial tokens and then answers. To stabilize depth token generation, we introduce novel composite depth-token objectives (marker, token, and count losses) and a soft-merging technique for differentiable reconstruction. We adopt a multi-task co-training strategy across diverse datasets, letting the model learn perception tokens to tackle multiple downstream tasks. Building on InternVL, Perceptio achieves state-of-the-art performance across benchmarks: improving referring expression segmentation by +0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU on RefCOCO/+/g HardBLINK spatial understanding accuracy by 10.3%, and MMBench accuracy by 1.0%, demonstrating that explicit spatial chain-of-thought materially strengthens spatial grounding in LVLMs.
tldr: Perceptio enhances LVLMs with explicit 2D/3D spatial reasoning by incorporating semantic segmentation and depth tokens, achieving state-of-the-art performance on spatial grounding and visual reasoning benchmarks.
tldr_zh: Perceptio通过结合语义分割和深度tokens，增强了LVLMs的2D/3D空间推理能力，并在空间定位和视觉推理基准测试中取得了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 大型视觉语言模型(LVLMs)在语义理解方面表现出色，但在精细空间定位方面存在困难，因为模型必须隐式地推断复杂的几何结构，而从不产生空间解释。 我们提出了Perceptio，一种感知增强的LVLM，具有2D和3D空间推理能力，通过在自回归序列中直接生成的显式语义分割token和深度token来实现。 具体而言，我们（i）从一个强大的单目教师模型中提炼出一个VQVAE深度码本，将密集深度信息标记化为紧凑的序列，以及（ii）将基于SAM2的语义分割token和VQ-VAE深度token集成到LLM中，以便模型首先生成空间token，然后回答问题。 为了稳定深度token的生成，我们引入了新颖的复合深度token目标（标记、token和计数损失）以及用于可微重建的软合并技术。我们采用跨多样化数据集的多任务协同训练策略，让模型学习感知token来处理多个下游任务。 在InternVL的基础上，Perceptio在多个基准测试中实现了最先进的性能：在RefCOCO/+/g上将指代表达式分割的cIoU提高了+0.8/+1.4/+1.1，将HardBLINK空间理解准确率提高了10.3%，并将MMBench的准确率提高了1.0%，这表明显式的空间思维链显著增强了LVLMs中的空间定位能力。

title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents
summary:
We introduce \emph{Memento-Skills}, a generalist, continually-learnable LLM agent system that functions as an \emph{agent-designing agent}: it autonomously constructs, adapts, and improves task-specific agents through experience. The system is built on a memory-based reinforcement learning framework with \emph{stateful prompts}, where reusable skills (stored as structured markdown files) serve as persistent, evolving memory. These skills encode both behaviour and context, enabling the agent to carry forward knowledge across interactions.
  Starting from simple elementary skills (like Web search and terminal operations), the agent continually improves via the \emph{Read--Write Reflective Learning} mechanism introduced in \emph{Memento~2}~\cite{wang2025memento2}. In the \emph{read} phase, a behaviour-trainable skill router selects the most relevant skill conditioned on the current stateful prompt; in the \emph{write} phase, the agent updates and expands its skill library based on new experience. This closed-loop design enables \emph{continual learning without updating LLM parameters}, as all adaptation is realised through the evolution of externalised skills and prompts.
  Unlike prior approaches that rely on human-designed agents, Memento-Skills enables a generalist agent to \emph{design agents end-to-end} for new tasks. Through iterative skill generation and refinement, the system progressively improves its own capabilities. Experiments on the \emph{General AI Assistants} benchmark and \emph{Humanity's Last Exam} demonstrate sustained gains, achieving 26.2\% and 116.2\% relative improvements in overall accuracy, respectively. Code is available at https://github.com/Memento-Teams/Memento-Skills.
tldr: The paper introduces Memento-Skills, a continuously learning LLM agent system that autonomously designs and improves task-specific agents through a memory-based reinforcement learning framework using evolving skills. Experiments show significant performance improvements on general AI benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 Memento-Skills，一个持续学习的 LLM 智能体系统，它通过基于记忆的强化学习框架和不断演进的技能来自主设计和改进特定任务的智能体。实验表明，在通用 AI 基准测试中，性能得到了显著提升。
summary_zh:
我们引入了\emph{Memento-Skills}，一个通用的、可连续学习的LLM智能体系统，它充当着一个\emph{智能体-设计智能体}的角色：它通过经验自主地构建、调整和改进特定任务的智能体。该系统构建于一个基于记忆的强化学习框架上，采用\emph{状态化提示}，其中可重用的技能（存储为结构化的markdown文件）充当持久的、不断发展的记忆。这些技能既编码了行为也编码了上下文，使智能体能够跨交互传递知识。

从简单的基本技能（如Web搜索和终端操作）开始，智能体通过\emph{Memento~2}~\cite{wang2025memento2}中引入的\emph{读--写反思学习}机制不断改进。在\emph{读}阶段，一个行为可训练的技能路由器基于当前的状态化提示选择最相关的技能；在\emph{写}阶段，智能体基于新的经验更新并扩展其技能库。这种闭环设计实现了\emph{无需更新LLM参数的持续学习}，因为所有的适应都通过外部化技能和提示的演变来实现。

与依赖于人工设计的智能体的先前方法不同，Memento-Skills使通用智能体能够为新任务\emph{端到端地设计智能体}。通过迭代的技能生成和优化，该系统逐步提高自身的能力。\emph{通用AI助手}基准测试和\emph{人类的最后测试}的实验表明，系统取得了持续的提升，分别在总体准确率上实现了26.2%和116.2%的相对改进。代码可在https://github.com/Memento-Teams/Memento-Skills获取。

title: Ontology-Guided Diffusion for Zero-Shot Visual Sim2Real Transfer
summary: Bridging the simulation-to-reality (sim2real) gap remains challenging as labelled real-world data is scarce. Existing diffusion-based approaches rely on unstructured prompts or statistical alignment, which do not capture the structured factors that make images look real. We introduce Ontology- Guided Diffusion (OGD), a neuro-symbolic zero-shot sim2real image translation framework that represents realism as structured knowledge. OGD decomposes realism into an ontology of interpretable traits -- such as lighting and material properties -- and encodes their relationships in a knowledge graph. From a synthetic image, OGD infers trait activations and uses a graph neural network to produce a global embedding. In parallel, a symbolic planner uses the ontology traits to compute a consistent sequence of visual edits needed to narrow the realism gap. The graph embedding conditions a pretrained instruction-guided diffusion model via cross-attention, while the planned edits are converted into a structured instruction prompt. Across benchmarks, our graph-based embeddings better distinguish real from synthetic imagery than baselines, and OGD outperforms state-of-the-art diffusion methods in sim2real image translations. Overall, OGD shows that explicitly encoding realism structure enables interpretable, data-efficient, and generalisable zero-shot sim2real transfer.
tldr: The paper introduces Ontology-Guided Diffusion (OGD), a zero-shot sim2real image translation framework that uses an ontology of interpretable traits and a graph neural network to guide a diffusion model, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了本体引导扩散（OGD），一种零样本的sim2real图像转换框架，它使用可解释特征的本体和一个图神经网络来引导扩散模型，实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 弥合仿真到现实（sim2real）的差距仍然具有挑战性，因为带标签的真实世界数据稀缺。现有的基于扩散的方法依赖于非结构化提示或统计对齐，无法捕捉使图像看起来真实的结构化因素。我们引入了本体引导扩散（OGD），这是一个神经符号零样本sim2real图像翻译框架，它将真实感表示为结构化知识。OGD将真实感分解为可解释的特征本体——例如光照和材质属性——并在知识图中编码它们的关系。从合成图像中，OGD推断出特征激活，并使用图神经网络来生成全局嵌入。同时，符号规划器使用本体特征来计算缩小真实感差距所需的一致视觉编辑序列。图嵌入通过交叉注意力有条件地控制预训练的指令引导扩散模型，而计划的编辑被转换为结构化指令提示。在各个基准测试中，我们的基于图的嵌入比基线更好地区分了真实图像和合成图像，并且OGD在sim2real图像翻译中优于最先进的扩散方法。总而言之，OGD表明，显式地编码真实感结构能够实现可解释的、数据高效的且可泛化的零样本sim2real迁移。

title: MemMA: Coordinating the Memory Cycle through Multi-Agent Reasoning and In-Situ Self-Evolution
summary: Memory-augmented LLM agents maintain external memory banks to support long-horizon interaction, yet most existing systems treat construction, retrieval, and utilization as isolated subroutines. This creates two coupled challenges: strategic blindness on the forward path of the memory cycle, where construction and retrieval are driven by local heuristics rather than explicit strategic reasoning, and sparse, delayed supervision on the backward path, where downstream failures rarely translate into direct repairs of the memory bank. To address these challenges, we propose MemMA, a plug-and-play multi-agent framework that coordinates the memory cycle along both the forward and backward paths. On the forward path, a Meta-Thinker produces structured guidance that steers a Memory Manager during construction and directs a Query Reasoner during iterative retrieval. On the backward path, MemMA introduces in-situ self-evolving memory construction, which synthesizes probe QA pairs, verifies the current memory, and converts failures into repair actions before the memory is finalized. Extensive experiments on LoCoMo show that MemMA consistently outperforms existing baselines across multiple LLM backbones and improves three different storage backends in a plug-and-play manner. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ventr1c/memma.
tldr: The paper introduces MemMA, a multi-agent framework that coordinates the memory cycle in memory-augmented LLM agents by using explicit strategic reasoning on the forward path and in-situ self-evolving memory construction on the backward path, demonstrating improvements on the LoCoMo benchmark.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MemMA，一个多智能体框架，通过在正向路径上使用显式策略推理和在反向路径上使用原位自演化记忆构建来协调记忆增强LLM代理中的记忆周期，并在LoCoMo基准测试中展示了改进。
summary_zh: 记忆增强型LLM智能体维护外部记忆库以支持长时交互，然而，现有的大多数系统将构建、检索和利用视为孤立的子程序。这产生了两个相互关联的挑战：记忆循环前向路径上的策略盲视，其中构建和检索由局部启发式方法驱动，而非显式的策略推理；以及后向路径上的稀疏、滞后监督，其中下游的失败很少转化为对记忆库的直接修复。为应对这些挑战，我们提出MemMA，一种即插即用的多智能体框架，协调记忆循环的前向和后向路径。在前向路径上，元思考器产生结构化指导，引导记忆管理者在构建期间进行操作，并指导查询推理器进行迭代检索。在后向路径上，MemMA引入原位自进化记忆构建，该方法合成探测QA对，验证当前记忆，并将失败转化为修复行动，而后才最终确定记忆。在LoCoMo上的大量实验表明，MemMA在多个LLM骨干网络上始终优于现有基线，并以即插即用的方式改进了三种不同的存储后端。我们的代码已公开，地址为https://github.com/ventr1c/memma。

title: HISR: Hindsight Information Modulated Segmental Process Rewards For Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
summary: While large language models excel in diverse domains, their performance on complex longhorizon agentic decision-making tasks remains limited. Most existing methods concentrate on designing effective reward models (RMs) to advance performance via multi-turn reinforcement learning. However, they suffer from delayed propagation in sparse outcome rewards and unreliable credit assignment with potentially overly fine-grained and unfocused turnlevel process rewards. In this paper, we propose (HISR) exploiting Hindsight Information to modulate Segmental process Rewards, which closely aligns rewards with sub-goals and underscores significant segments to enhance the reliability of credit assignment. Specifically, a segment-level process RM is presented to assign rewards for each sub-goal in the task, avoiding excessively granular allocation to turns. To emphasize significant segments in the trajectory, a hindsight model is devised to reflect the preference of performing a certain action after knowing the trajectory outcome. With this characteristic, we design the ratios of sequence likelihoods between hindsight and policy model to measure action importance. The ratios are subsequently employed to aggregate segment importance scores, which in turn modulate segmental process rewards, enhancing credit assignment reliability. Extensive experimental results on three publicly benchmarks demonstrate the validity of our method.
tldr: The paper introduces HISR, a method that employs hindsight information to modulate segmental process rewards in multi-turn reinforcement learning, improving credit assignment by focusing on significant trajectory segments and aligning rewards with sub-goals. Experiments on benchmarks demonstrate its effectiveness.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种名为HISR的方法，该方法利用后见之明信息来调节多轮强化学习中的分段过程奖励，通过关注重要的轨迹分段并将奖励与子目标对齐来改进信用分配。在多个基准测试上的实验证明了其有效性。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型在各个领域表现出色，但在复杂、长期的自主决策任务上的性能仍然有限。现有方法大多专注于设计有效的奖励模型(RM)，以通过多轮强化学习来提升性能。然而，这些方法存在稀疏结果奖励的延迟传播问题，以及可能过于细粒度和不集中的轮级别过程奖励所带来的不可靠的信用分配问题。在本文中，我们提出(HISR)，利用后见之明信息来调节分段过程奖励，这使奖励与子目标紧密对齐，并强调重要分段，从而增强信用分配的可靠性。具体而言，我们提出一个分段级别的过程RM，为任务中的每个子目标分配奖励，避免对轮级别进行过度细粒度的分配。为了强调轨迹中的重要分段，我们设计了一个后见之明模型，以反映在已知轨迹结果后执行特定动作的偏好。基于这一特性，我们设计了后见之明模型和策略模型之间的序列似然比，以衡量动作的重要性。该比率随后用于聚合片段重要性得分，进而调节分段过程奖励，从而提高信用分配的可靠性。在三个公开基准测试上的大量实验结果证明了我们方法的有效性。

title: Thinking with Constructions: A Benchmark and Policy Optimization for Visual-Text Interleaved Geometric Reasoning
summary: Geometric reasoning inherently requires "thinking with constructions" -- the dynamic manipulation of visual aids to bridge the gap between problem conditions and solutions. However, existing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are largely confined to passive inference with static diagrams, lacking the strategic knowledge of when and how to construct effective visual aids. To address this, we present a framework for Visual-Text Interleaved Chain-of-Thought. We first introduce GeoAux-Bench, the first benchmark comprising 4,334 geometry problems that aligns textual construction steps with ground-truth visual updates. Our pilot study reveals two critical insights: (1) interleaved visual-textual aids outperform single-modality counterparts, which cannot losslessly capture geometric synergy; and (2) valid constructions act as entropy reducers, strongly correlating with reduced reasoning perplexity. Building on these findings, we propose Action Applicability Policy Optimization (A2PO), a reinforcement learning paradigm for mastering strategic construction. A2PO employs Adaptive Reward Shaping to regulate the timing and quality of visual aids via counterfactual sampling to distinguish necessary from redundant constructions. Experiments demonstrate our approach enables MLLMs to leverage selective auxiliary constructions, yielding a 3.51% gain over strong baselines. Code and data are available on GitHub.
tldr: The paper introduces GeoAux-Bench, a new benchmark for geometric reasoning requiring visual constructions, and proposes Action Applicability Policy Optimization (A2PO), a reinforcement learning method that enables MLLMs to strategically use visual aids, achieving improved performance.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个新的几何推理基准 GeoAux-Bench，它要求进行视觉构造，并提出了动作适用性策略优化 (A2PO)，这是一种强化学习方法，使 MLLM 能够战略性地使用视觉辅助工具，从而提高性能。
summary_zh: 几何推理本质上需要“通过构造进行思考”——动态地操纵视觉辅助工具以弥合问题条件和解决方案之间的差距。然而，现有的多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）主要局限于对静态图表的被动推理，缺乏何时以及如何构造有效的视觉辅助工具的策略性知识。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一个用于视觉-文本交错思维链的框架。我们首先推出了GeoAux-Bench，这是第一个包含4,334个几何问题的基准测试，它将文本构造步骤与真实视觉更新对齐。我们的初步研究揭示了两个关键见解：（1）交错的视觉-文本辅助优于单模态辅助，后者无法无损地捕捉几何协同作用; (2) 有效的构造充当熵减少器，与降低推理困惑度密切相关。 基于这些发现，我们提出了行动适用性策略优化（A2PO），这是一种用于掌握策略性构造的强化学习范式。 A2PO采用自适应奖励塑造，通过反事实抽样来区分必要和冗余构造，从而调节视觉辅助工具的时机和质量。实验表明，我们的方法使MLLMs能够利用选择性的辅助构造，相较于强大的基线提高了3.51%。代码和数据可在GitHub上找到。

title: Balanced Thinking: Improving Chain of Thought Training in Vision Language Models
summary: Multimodal reasoning in vision-language models (VLMs) typically relies on a two-stage process: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). In standard SFT, all tokens contribute equally to the loss, even though reasoning data are inherently token-imbalanced. Long <think> traces overshadow short but task-critical <answer> segments, leading to verbose reasoning and inaccurate answers. We propose SCALe (Scheduled Curriculum Adaptive Loss), which explicitly separates supervision over reasoning and answer segments using dynamic, length-independent weighting. Unlike vanilla SFT, which overweights the <think> segment, SCALe-SFT gradually shifts the focus from <think> to <answer> throughout training via a cosine scheduling policy, encouraging concise and well-grounded reasoning. We evaluate SCALe across diverse benchmarks and architectures. Results show that SCALe consistently improves accuracy over vanilla SFT and matches the performance of the full two-phase SFT + GRPO pipeline while requiring only about one-seventh of the training time, making it a lightweight yet effective alternative. When combined with GRPO, SCALe achieves the best overall performance, highlighting its value both as a standalone method and as a strong foundation for reinforcement refinement.
tldr: This paper introduces SCALe, a novel training method for Vision-Language Models that balances the supervision of reasoning and answer segments during SFT, achieving comparable performance to SFT+GRPO with significantly less training time and further improving results when combined with GRPO.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为SCALe 的视觉语言模型训练新方法，该方法在SFT期间平衡了推理和答案部分的监督，以显著减少的训练时间实现了与SFT+GRPO相当的性能，并且在与GRPO结合使用时进一步提高了结果。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLM）中的多模态推理通常依赖于两阶段过程：监督微调（SFT）和强化学习（RL）。在标准SFT中，所有token对损失的贡献相同，尽管推理数据本质上存在token不平衡。过长的<think>轨迹掩盖了短小但对任务至关重要的<answer>片段，导致冗长的推理和不准确的回答。我们提出SCALe（Scheduled Curriculum Adaptive Loss，调度课程自适应损失），它使用动态的、与长度无关的权重，显式地分离对推理和答案片段的监督。与过度加权<think>片段的原始SFT不同，SCALe-SFT通过余弦调度策略，在整个训练过程中逐渐将焦点从<think>转移到<answer>，从而鼓励简洁且具有良好基础依据的推理。我们在不同的基准和架构上评估了SCALe。结果表明，SCALe始终优于原始SFT，并且在仅需约七分之一的训练时间的情况下，达到完整两阶段SFT + GRPO流程的性能，使其成为一种轻量级但有效的替代方案。当与GRPO结合使用时，SCALe实现了最佳的整体性能，突显了其作为独立方法以及作为强化精炼的坚实基础的价值。

title: D-Mem: A Dual-Process Memory System for LLM Agents
summary: Driven by the development of persistent, self-adapting autonomous agents, equipping these systems with high-fidelity memory access for long-horizon reasoning has emerged as a critical requirement. However, prevalent retrieval-based memory frameworks often follow an incremental processing paradigm that continuously extracts and updates conversational memories into vector databases, relying on semantic retrieval when queried. While this approach is fast, it inherently relies on lossy abstraction, frequently missing contextually critical information and struggling to resolve queries that rely on fine-grained contextual understanding. To address this, we introduce D-Mem, a dual-process memory system. It retains lightweight vector retrieval for routine queries while establishing an exhaustive Full Deliberation module as a high-fidelity fallback. To achieve cognitive economy without sacrificing accuracy, D-Mem employs a Multi-dimensional Quality Gating policy to dynamically bridge these two processes. Experiments on the LoCoMo and RealTalk benchmarks using GPT-4o-mini and Qwen3-235B-Instruct demonstrate the efficacy of our approach. Notably, our Multi-dimensional Quality Gating policy achieves an F1 score of 53.5 on LoCoMo with GPT-4o-mini. This outperforms our static retrieval baseline, Mem0$^\ast$ (51.2), and recovers 96.7\% of the Full Deliberation's performance (55.3), while incurring significantly lower computational costs.
tldr: The paper introduces D-Mem, a dual-process memory system for LLM agents that combines efficient retrieval with a high-fidelity deliberation module, dynamically managed by a quality-gating policy, to improve performance on long-horizon reasoning tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为D-Mem的双过程记忆系统，用于LLM Agent。该系统结合了高效的检索和高保真度的审议模块，并通过质量门控策略动态管理，从而提高长期推理任务的性能。
summary_zh: 在持续、自适应自主智能体的发展驱动下，为这些系统配备用于长时程推理的高保真内存访问已成为一项关键需求。然而，目前流行的基于检索的内存框架通常遵循一种增量处理范式，持续地将对话记忆提取并更新到向量数据库中，并在查询时依赖语义检索。虽然这种方法速度很快，但它本质上依赖于有损抽象，经常遗漏在上下文中至关重要的信息，并且难以解析依赖于细粒度上下文理解的查询。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了D-Mem，一种双进程内存系统。它保留了轻量级向量检索用于常规查询，同时建立了一个详尽的完整审议模块作为高保真回退方案。为了在不牺牲准确性的前提下实现认知经济性，D-Mem采用了一种多维质量门控策略来动态连接这两个进程。使用GPT-4o-mini和Qwen3-235B-Instruct在LoCoMo和RealTalk基准测试上的实验证明了我们方法的有效性。值得注意的是，我们的多维质量门控策略在LoCoMo上使用GPT-4o-mini实现了53.5的F1分数。这优于我们的静态检索基线Mem0$^\ast$ (51.2)，并恢复了完整审议性能的96.7% (55.3)，同时产生了显著更低的计算成本。

title: Agentic Flow Steering and Parallel Rollout Search for Spatially Grounded Text-to-Image Generation
summary: Precise Text-to-Image (T2I) generation has achieved great success but is hindered by the limited relational reasoning of static text encoders and the error accumulation in open-loop sampling. Without real-time feedback, initial semantic ambiguities during the Ordinary Differential Equation trajectory inevitably escalate into stochastic deviations from spatial constraints. To bridge this gap, we introduce AFS-Search (Agentic Flow Steering and Parallel Rollout Search), a training-free closed-loop framework built upon FLUX.1-dev. AFS-Search incorporates a training-free closed-loop parallel rollout search and flow steering mechanism, which leverages a Vision-Language Model (VLM) as a semantic critic to diagnose intermediate latents and dynamically steer the velocity field via precise spatial grounding. Complementarily, we formulate T2I generation as a sequential decision-making process, exploring multiple trajectories through lookahead simulations and selecting the optimal path based on VLM-guided rewards. Further, we provide AFS-Search-Pro for higher performance and AFS-Search-Fast for quicker generation. Experimental results show that our AFS-Search-Pro greatly boosts the performance of the original FLUX.1-dev, achieving state-of-the-art results across three different benchmarks. Meanwhile, AFS-Search-Fast also significantly enhances performance while maintaining fast generation speed.
tldr: The paper presents AFS-Search, a training-free closed-loop framework that uses a Vision-Language Model to guide text-to-image generation through parallel rollout search and flow steering, achieving state-of-the-art results.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了AFS-Search，一个无需训练的闭环框架，它利用视觉-语言模型通过并行rollout搜索和流向引导来指导文本到图像的生成，并实现了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 精确的文本到图像（T2I）生成已经取得了巨大的成功，但受到静态文本编码器有限的关系推理能力和开环采样中误差累积的限制。在缺乏实时反馈的情况下，常微分方程轨迹中的初始语义歧义不可避免地会升级为对空间约束的随机偏离。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了AFS-Search（代理式流向引导和平行展开搜索），这是一个基于FLUX.1-dev构建的免训练闭环框架。AFS-Search包含一个免训练闭环平行展开搜索和流向引导机制，该机制利用视觉-语言模型（VLM）作为语义评论员来诊断中间潜变量，并通过精确的空间定位动态地引导速度场。作为补充，我们将文本到图像生成公式化为一个顺序决策过程，通过前瞻模拟探索多条轨迹，并基于VLM引导的奖励选择最优路径。此外，我们提供了用于更高性能的AFS-Search-Pro和用于更快生成的AFS-Search-Fast。实验结果表明，我们的AFS-Search-Pro极大地提升了原始FLUX.1-dev的性能，在三个不同的基准测试中实现了最先进的结果。与此同时，AFS-Search-Fast在保持快速生成速度的同时也显著提高了性能。

title: OpenT2M: No-frill Motion Generation with Open-source,Large-scale, High-quality Data
summary: Text-to-motion (T2M) generation aims to create realistic human movements from text descriptions, with promising applications in animation and robotics. Despite recent progress, current T2M models perform poorly on unseen text descriptions due to the small scale and limited diversity of existing motion datasets. To address this problem, we introduce OpenT2M, a million-level, high-quality, and open-source motion dataset containing over 2800 hours of human motion. Each sequence undergoes rigorous quality control through physical feasibility validation and multi-granularity filtering, with detailed second-wise text annotations. We also develop an automated pipeline for creating long-horizon sequences, enabling complex motion generation. Building upon OpenT2M, we introduce MonoFrill, a pretrained motion model that achieves compelling T2M results without complicated designs or technique tricks as "frills". Its core component is 2D-PRQ, a novel motion tokenizer that captures spatiotemporal dependencies by dividing the human body into biology parts. Experiments show that OpenT2M significantly improves generalization of existing T2M models, while 2D-PRQ achieves superior reconstruction and strong zero-shot performance. We expect OpenT2M and MonoFrill will advance the T2M field by addressing longstanding data quality and benchmarking challenges.
tldr: This paper introduces OpenT2M, a large-scale, high-quality, open-source dataset for text-to-motion generation and MonoFrill, a simple yet effective T2M model that utilizes a novel motion tokenizer, 2D-PRQ, achieving strong zero-shot performance.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了OpenT2M，一个大规模、高质量、开源的文本到运动生成数据集，以及MonoFrill，一个简单而有效的T2M模型，该模型利用了一种新的运动标记器2D-PRQ，实现了强大的零样本性能。
summary_zh: 文本到运动 (T2M) 生成旨在从文本描述中创建逼真的人体运动，在动画和机器人领域具有广阔的应用前景。尽管最近取得了一些进展，但由于现有运动数据集规模小且多样性有限，目前的 T2M 模型在未见过的文本描述上表现不佳。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 OpenT2M，一个百万级别、高质量且开源的运动数据集，包含超过 2800 小时的人体运动。每个序列都经过严格的质量控制，包括物理可行性验证和多粒度过滤，并配有详细的逐秒文本标注。我们还开发了一个自动化的流水线，用于创建长时程序列，从而实现复杂的运动生成。在 OpenT2M 的基础上，我们推出了 MonoFrill，一种预训练运动模型，它无需复杂的构架设计或技术技巧（即“繁文缛节”）即可实现引人注目的 T2M 结果。其核心组件是 2D-PRQ，这是一种新型运动标记器，它通过将人体划分为生物学部分来捕获时空依赖性。实验表明，OpenT2M 显著提高了现有 T2M 模型的泛化能力，而 2D-PRQ 实现了卓越的重建效果和强大的零样本性能。我们期望 OpenT2M 和 MonoFrill 通过解决长期存在的数据质量和基准测试挑战来推动 T2M 领域的发展。

title: ZEBRAARENA: A Diagnostic Simulation Environment for Studying Reasoning-Action Coupling in Tool-Augmented LLMs
summary: Tool-augmented large language models (LLMs) must tightly couple multi-step reasoning with external actions, yet existing benchmarks often confound this interplay with complex environment dynamics, memorized knowledge or dataset contamination. In this paper, we introduce ZebraArena, a procedurally generated diagnostic environment for studying reasoning-action coupling in tool-augmented LLMs, with controllable difficulty and a knowledge-minimal design, which limits gains from memorization or dataset contamination. Each task in ZebraArena requires a set of critical information which is available only through targeted tool use, yielding an interpretable interface between external information acquisition and deductive reasoning. This design provides deterministic evaluation via unique solutions, and a theoretical optimal query count for measuring efficient tool use. We show that ZebraArena requires a combination of in-depth reasoning and accurate external tool calling, which remains a challenge as frontier reasoning models such as GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Pro only achieves 60% accuracy on the hard instances. We also observe a persistent gaps between theoretical optimality and practical tool usage. For example, GPT-5 uses 70-270% more tool calls than the theoretical optimum. We highlight the key findings in our evaluation, and hope ZebraArena stimulates further research on the interplay between internal reasoning and external action.
tldr: The paper introduces ZebraArena, a procedurally generated diagnostic environment designed to test reasoning-action coupling in tool-augmented LLMs, revealing limitations in even state-of-the-art models and highlighting inefficiencies in tool usage.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 ZebraArena，一个程序生成的诊断环境，旨在测试工具增强型 LLM 中的推理-行动耦合。研究表明即使是最先进的模型也存在局限性，并突出了工具使用方面的效率低下。
summary_zh: 工具增强的大型语言模型 (LLMs) 必须将多步推理与外部行动紧密结合，然而现有的基准测试常常将这种相互作用与复杂的环境动态、记忆知识或数据集污染混淆。 在本文中，我们引入了 ZebraArena，这是一个程序化生成的诊断环境，用于研究工具增强型 LLMs 中的推理-行动耦合，具有可控的难度和知识最小化的设计，从而限制了记忆或数据集污染带来的收益。 ZebraArena 中的每个任务都需要一组关键信息，这些信息只能通过有针对性的工具使用获得，从而在外部信息获取和演绎推理之间产生一个可解释的接口。 这种设计通过唯一的解决方案提供确定性的评估，并提供一个理论上的最优查询计数，用于衡量高效的工具使用。 我们表明，ZebraArena 需要深入的推理和准确的外部工具调用相结合，这仍然是一个挑战，因为像 GPT-5 和 Gemini 2.5 Pro 这样的前沿推理模型在困难的实例上仅达到 60% 的准确率。 我们还观察到理论最优性和实际工具使用之间存在持续的差距。 例如，GPT-5 比理论上的最佳值多使用 70-270% 的工具调用。 我们重点介绍评估中的关键发现，并希望 ZebraArena 能够激发更多关于内部推理和外部行动之间相互作用的研究。

title: Counting Circuits: Mechanistic Interpretability of Visual Reasoning in Large Vision-Language Models
summary: Counting serves as a simple but powerful test of a Large Vision-Language Model's (LVLM's) reasoning; it forces the model to identify each individual object and then add them all up. In this study, we investigate how LVLMs implement counting using controlled synthetic and real-world benchmarks, combined with mechanistic analyses. Our results show that LVLMs display a human-like counting behavior, with precise performance on small numerosities and noisy estimation for larger quantities. We introduce two novel interpretability methods, Visual Activation Patching and HeadLens, and use them to uncover a structured "counting circuit" that is largely shared across a variety of visual reasoning tasks. Building on these insights, we propose a lightweight intervention strategy that exploits simple and abundantly available synthetic images to fine-tune arbitrary pretrained LVLMs exclusively on counting. Despite the narrow scope of this fine-tuning, the intervention not only enhances counting accuracy on in-distribution synthetic data, but also yields an average improvement of +8.36% on out-of-distribution counting benchmarks and an average gain of +1.54% on complex, general visual reasoning tasks for Qwen2.5-VL. These findings highlight the central, influential role of counting in visual reasoning and suggest a potential pathway for improving overall visual reasoning capabilities through targeted enhancement of counting mechanisms.
tldr: This paper investigates how Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) perform counting, identifies a counting circuit using novel interpretability methods, and improves general visual reasoning by fine-tuning on synthetic counting data.
tldr_zh: 本文研究了大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）如何执行计数任务，利用新的可解释性方法识别了一个计数电路，并通过在合成计数数据上进行微调来提高一般的视觉推理能力。
summary_zh: 计数是评估大型视觉-语言模型（LVLM）推理能力的一种简单而强大的测试方法；它迫使模型识别每个单独的物体，然后将它们全部加起来。在本研究中，我们结合受控的合成数据集和真实世界基准，以及机制分析，来研究LVLM如何实现计数。我们的结果表明，LVLM表现出类似人类的计数行为，在小数量上表现出精确的性能，而在较大数量上则表现出嘈杂的估计。我们引入了两种新的可解释性方法：视觉激活修补和 HeadLens，并利用它们揭示了一个结构化的“计数回路”，该回路在各种视觉推理任务中得到广泛共享。基于这些见解，我们提出了一种轻量级的干预策略，该策略利用简单且大量可用的合成图像，专门针对计数对任意预训练的LVLM进行微调。尽管这种微调范围狭窄，但该干预不仅提高了模型在同分布合成数据上的计数准确性，还在异分布计数基准上平均提升了+8.36%，并且对Qwen2.5-VL的复杂、通用视觉推理任务平均提升了+1.54%。这些发现突出了计数在视觉推理中的核心和有影响力的作用，并为通过有针对性地增强计数机制来提高整体视觉推理能力提供了一种潜在途径。

title: Cross-Domain Demo-to-Code via Neurosymbolic Counterfactual Reasoning
summary: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have enabled video-instructed robotic programming, allowing agents to interpret video demonstrations and generate executable control code. We formulate video-instructed robotic programming as a cross-domain adaptation problem, where perceptual and physical differences between demonstration and deployment induce procedural mismatches. However, current VLMs lack the procedural understanding needed to reformulate causal dependencies and achieve task-compatible behavior under such domain shifts. We introduce NeSyCR, a neurosymbolic counterfactual reasoning framework that enables verifiable adaptation of task procedures, providing a reliable synthesis of code policies. NeSyCR abstracts video demonstrations into symbolic trajectories that capture the underlying task procedure. Given deployment observations, it derives counterfactual states that reveal cross-domain incompatibilities. By exploring the symbolic state space with verifiable checks, NeSyCR proposes procedural revisions that restore compatibility with the demonstrated procedure. NeSyCR achieves a 31.14% improvement in task success over the strongest baseline Statler, showing robust cross-domain adaptation across both simulated and real-world manipulation tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces NeSyCR, a neurosymbolic counterfactual reasoning framework, for cross-domain video-instructed robotic programming, which adapts task procedures by addressing perceptual and physical differences between demonstration and deployment. It achieves significant improvement in task success over existing methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种神经符号反事实推理框架NeSyCR，用于跨域视频指导机器人编程，通过解决演示和部署之间感知和物理差异来调整任务过程。 它在任务成功率方面实现了相对于现有方法的显着提升。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的最新进展推动了视频指导机器人编程的发展，使得智能体能够解释视频演示并生成可执行的控制代码。我们将视频指导机器人编程建模为一个跨域自适应问题，其中演示和部署之间的感知和物理差异会导致程序上的不匹配。然而，当前的VLMs缺乏必要的程序理解能力来重构因果依赖关系，并在这种领域转移下实现任务兼容的行为。我们引入了NeSyCR，一个神经符号反事实推理框架，它能够对任务流程进行可验证的自适应，从而可靠地合成代码策略。NeSyCR将视频演示抽象成符号轨迹，以捕捉底层的任务流程。给定部署观测，它推导出揭示了跨域不兼容性的反事实状态。通过探索具有可验证检查的符号状态空间，NeSyCR提出程序修订建议，以恢复与演示流程的兼容性。NeSyCR在任务成功率上比最强的基线模型Statler提高了31.14%，展示了在模拟和真实世界操作任务中的鲁棒跨域自适应能力。

title: Discounted Beta--Bernoulli Reward Estimation for Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as an effective post-training paradigm for improving the reasoning capabilities of large language models. However, existing group-based RLVR methods often suffer from severe sample inefficiency. This inefficiency stems from reliance on point estimation of rewards from a small number of rollouts, leading to high estimation variance, variance collapse, and ineffective utilization of generated responses. In this work, we reformulate RLVR from a statistical estimation perspective by modeling rewards as samples drawn from a policy-induced distribution and casting advantage computation as the problem of estimating the reward distribution from finite data. Building on this view, we propose Discounted Beta--Bernoulli (DBB) reward estimation, which leverages historical reward statistics for the non-stationary distribution. Although biased, the resulting estimator exhibits reduced and stable variance, theoretically avoids estimated variance collapse, and achieves lower mean squared error than standard point estimation. Extensive experiments across six in-distribution and three out-of-distribution reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that GRPO with DBB consistently outperforms naive GRPO, achieving average Acc@8 improvements of 3.22/2.42 points in-distribution and 12.49/6.92 points out-of-distribution on the 1.7B and 8B models, respectively, without additional computational cost or memory usage.
tldr: This paper introduces Discounted Beta--Bernoulli (DBB) reward estimation for sample-efficient Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) in large language models, achieving improved performance compared to standard point estimation methods, especially in out-of-distribution scenarios.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了折扣 Beta--伯努利 (DBB) 奖励估计方法，用于大型语言模型中样本高效的带有可验证奖励的强化学习 (RLVR)，与标准点估计方法相比，尤其是在分布外场景中，实现了改进的性能。
summary_zh: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）已经成为一种有效的后训练范式，用于提升大型语言模型的推理能力。然而，现有的基于群组的RLVR方法通常存在严重的样本效率低下问题。这种低效率源于对少量rollout奖励的点估计的依赖，导致高估计方差、方差崩溃以及生成响应的无效利用。在这项工作中，我们从统计估计的角度重新构建RLVR，将奖励建模为从策略诱导分布中抽取的样本，并将优势计算转化为从有限数据中估计奖励分布的问题。基于这一观点，我们提出了折扣Beta-伯努利（DBB）奖励估计，它利用非平稳分布的历史奖励统计信息。虽然有偏，但由此产生的估计器表现出降低且稳定的方差，理论上避免了估计方差崩溃，并且实现了比标准点估计更低的均方误差。在六个分布内和三个分布外推理基准上的大量实验表明，采用DBB的GRPO始终优于朴素GRPO，在17亿和80亿参数模型上分别实现了分布内平均Acc@8提升3.22/2.42个点，分布外平均Acc@8提升12.49/6.92个点，而无需额外的计算成本或内存使用。

title: PlanTwin: Privacy-Preserving Planning Abstractions for Cloud-Assisted LLM Agents
summary:
Cloud-hosted large language models (LLMs) have become the de facto planners in agentic systems, coordinating tools and guiding execution over local environments. In many deployments, however, the environment being planned over is private, containing source code, files, credentials, and metadata that cannot be exposed to the cloud. Existing solutions address adjacent concerns, such as execution isolation, access control, or confidential inference, but they do not control what cloud planners observe during planning: within the permitted scope, \textit{raw environment state is still exposed}.
  We introduce PlanTwin, a privacy-preserving architecture for cloud-assisted planning without exposing raw local context. The key idea is to project the real environment into a \textit{planning-oriented digital twin}: a schema-constrained and de-identified abstract graph that preserves planning-relevant structure while removing reconstructable details. The cloud planner operates solely on this sanitized twin through a bounded capability interface, while a local gatekeeper enforces safety policies and cumulative disclosure budgets. We further formalize the privacy-utility trade-off as a capability granularity problem, define architectural privacy goals using $(k,δ)$-anonymity and $ε$-unlinkability, and mitigate compositional leakage through multi-turn disclosure control.
  We implement PlanTwin as middleware between local agents and cloud planners and evaluate it on 60 agentic tasks across ten domains with four cloud planners. PlanTwin achieves full sensitive-item non-disclosure (SND = 1.0) while maintaining planning quality close to full-context systems: three of four planners achieve PQS $> 0.79$, and the full pipeline incurs less than 2.2\% utility loss.
tldr: The paper introduces PlanTwin, a privacy-preserving architecture that allows cloud-based LLM planners to operate on a sanitized, abstract representation of a local environment, mitigating privacy risks while maintaining planning quality.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为 PlanTwin 的隐私保护架构，它允许基于云的 LLM 规划器在本地环境的经过清理和抽象化的表示上运行，从而在保持规划质量的同时降低隐私风险。
summary_zh:
云托管的大型语言模型（LLM）已成为代理系统中的事实规划器，协调工具并在本地环境中指导执行。然而，在许多部署中，所规划的环境是私有的，包含源代码、文件、凭据和元数据，这些数据不能暴露给云端。现有的解决方案解决了诸如执行隔离、访问控制或私密推理等相关问题，但它们并未控制云规划器在规划期间观察到的内容：在允许的范围内，\textit{原始环境状态仍然暴露}。

我们引入了PlanTwin，这是一种隐私保护架构，用于云辅助规划，同时不暴露原始本地上下文。其核心思想是将真实环境投影到一个\textit{面向规划的数字孪生}中：一个模式约束且去标识化的抽象图，该图保留了规划相关的结构，同时移除了可重建的细节。云规划器仅通过有界限的能力接口在此经过清理的孪生上运行，而本地门卫则强制执行安全策略和累积披露预算。我们进一步将隐私-效用权衡形式化为一个能力粒度问题，使用$(k,δ)$-匿名性和$ε$-不可链接性定义架构隐私目标，并通过多轮披露控制缓解组合泄露。

我们将PlanTwin实现为本地代理和云规划器之间的中间件，并在十个领域的60个代理任务中，使用四个云规划器对其进行评估。PlanTwin实现了完全的敏感项非披露（SND = 1.0），同时将规划质量保持在接近完整上下文系统的水平：四个规划器中有三个实现了PQS $> 0.79$，并且完整流水线的效用损失小于2.2%。

title: PowerFlow: Unlocking the Dual Nature of LLMs via Principled Distribution Matching
summary: Unsupervised Reinforcement Learning from Internal Feedback (RLIF) has emerged as a promising paradigm for eliciting the latent capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) without external supervision. However, current methods rely on heuristic intrinsic rewards, which often lack a well-defined theoretical optimization target and are prone to degenerative biases. In this work, we introduce PowerFlow, a principled framework that reformulates unsupervised fine-tuning as a distribution matching problem. By casting GFlowNet as an amortized variational sampler for unnormalized densities, we propose a length-aware Trajectory-Balance objective that explicitly neutralizes the structural length biases inherent in autoregressive generation. By targeting $α$-power distributions, PowerFlow enables the directional elicitation of the dual nature of LLMs: sharpening the distribution ($α> 1$) to intensify logical reasoning, or flattening it ($α< 1$) to unlock expressive creativity. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PowerFlow consistently outperforms existing RLIF methods, matching or even exceeding supervised GRPO. Furthermore, by mitigating over-sharpening in aligned models, our approach achieves simultaneous gains in diversity and quality, shifting the Pareto frontier in creative tasks.
tldr: PowerFlow introduces a novel unsupervised fine-tuning framework for LLMs based on distribution matching using GFlowNets and a length-aware Trajectory-Balance objective, enabling directional control over reasoning and creativity.
tldr_zh: PowerFlow 引入了一种新颖的 LLM 无监督微调框架，该框架基于使用 GFlowNets 的分布匹配和长度感知的轨迹平衡目标，从而能够定向控制推理和创造力。
summary_zh: 无监督内部反馈强化学习（RLIF）已成为一种有前景的范式，它能够在无需外部监督的情况下激发大型语言模型（LLM）的潜在能力。然而，目前的方法依赖于启发式内在奖励，这些奖励通常缺乏明确的理论优化目标，并且容易产生退化性偏差。本文介绍了一个原则性框架PowerFlow，它将无监督微调重新表述为一个分布匹配问题。通过将GFlowNet构建为非归一化密度的摊销变分采样器，我们提出了一种长度感知的轨迹平衡目标，该目标显式地中和了自回归生成中固有的结构长度偏差。通过以$α$-次幂分布为目标，PowerFlow能够定向地激发LLM的双重特性：锐化分布（$α> 1$）以增强逻辑推理能力，或平坦化分布（$α< 1$）以释放富有表现力的创造力。大量实验表明，PowerFlow始终优于现有的RLIF方法，甚至可以匹配或超过有监督的GRPO。此外，通过减轻对齐模型中的过度锐化，我们的方法实现了在多样性和质量方面的同步提升，从而在创意任务中移动了帕累托前沿。

title: MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer
summary:
Persistent Large Language Model (LLM) agents expose a critical governance gap in memory management. Standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks treat memory as passive storage, lacking mechanisms to resolve contradictions, enforce privacy, or prevent outdated information ("zombie memories") from contaminating the context window.
  We introduce MemArchitect, a governance layer that decouples memory lifecycle management from model weights. MemArchitect enforces explicit, rule-based policies, including memory decay, conflict resolution, and privacy controls.
  We demonstrate that governed memory consistently outperforms unmanaged memory in agentic settings, highlighting the necessity of structured memory governance for reliable and safe autonomous systems.
tldr: MemArchitect is a memory governance layer for persistent LLM agents, enforcing rule-based policies for memory decay, conflict resolution, and privacy, leading to improved performance in agentic settings compared to unmanaged memory.
tldr_zh: MemArchitect是一个用于持久性LLM代理的内存管理层，通过执行基于规则的策略（如内存衰减、冲突解决和隐私控制）来提升代理设置中的性能，优于非托管内存。
summary_zh:
持久化大型语言模型（LLM）代理暴露了内存管理方面的一个关键治理缺口。标准的检索增强生成（RAG）框架将内存视为被动存储，缺乏解决矛盾、执行隐私或防止过时信息（“僵尸记忆”）污染上下文窗口的机制。

我们提出了MemArchitect，一个将内存生命周期管理与模型权重解耦的治理层。MemArchitect执行显式的、基于规则的策略，包括记忆衰减、冲突解决和隐私控制。

我们证明了受治理的内存在代理环境中始终优于未受管理的内存，突显了结构化内存治理对于可靠和安全的自主系统的必要性。

title: Retrieval-Augmented LLM Agents: Learning to Learn from Experience
summary: While large language models (LLMs) have advanced the development of general-purpose agents, achieving robust generalization to unseen tasks remains a significant challenge. Current approaches typically rely on either fine-tuning or training-free memory-augmented generation using retrieved experience; yet both have limitations: fine-tuning often fails to extrapolate to new tasks, while experience retrieval often underperforms compared to supervised baselines. In this work, we propose to combine these approaches and systematically study how to train retrieval-augmented LLM agents to effectively leverage retrieved trajectories in-context. First, we establish a robust supervised fine-tuning (SFT) recipe using LoRA that outperforms several state-of-the-art agent training pipelines. Second, we provide a detailed analysis of key design choices for experience retrieval, identifying optimal strategies for storage, querying, and trajectory selection. Finally, we propose a pipeline that integrates experience retrieval into the fine-tuning process. Our results demonstrate that this combined approach significantly improves generalization to unseen tasks, providing a scalable and effective framework for building agents that learn to learn from experience.
tldr: This paper explores combining fine-tuning and experience retrieval for LLM agents, showing improved generalization to unseen tasks through a systematically trained retrieval-augmented approach.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了将微调和经验检索相结合的LLM智能体，通过系统训练的检索增强方法，展示了对未见任务的更好泛化能力。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）推动了通用代理的发展，但实现对未见任务的稳健泛化仍然是一项重大挑战。目前的方法通常依赖于微调或使用检索到的经验进行无训练的记忆增强生成；然而，这两者都有局限性：微调往往无法外推到新任务，而经验检索与监督基线相比通常表现不佳。在这项工作中，我们提出结合这些方法，并系统地研究如何训练检索增强型LLM代理，以有效地在上下文中利用检索到的轨迹。首先，我们建立了一个使用LoRA的稳健的监督微调（SFT）配方，其性能优于几种最先进的代理训练流程。其次，我们对经验检索的关键设计选择进行了详细分析，确定了存储、查询和轨迹选择的最佳策略。最后，我们提出一个将经验检索集成到微调过程中的流程。我们的结果表明，这种组合方法显著提高了对未见任务的泛化能力，为构建能够从经验中学习的学习型代理提供了一个可扩展且有效的框架。

title: Enactor: From Traffic Simulators to Surrogate World Models
summary: Traffic microsimulators are widely used to evaluate road network performance under various ``what-if" conditions. However, the behavior models controlling the actions of the actors are overly simplistic and fails to capture realistic actor-actor interactions. Deep learning-based methods have been applied to model vehicles and pedestrians as ``agents" responding to their surrounding ``environment" (including lanes, signals, and neighboring agents). Although effective in learning actor-actor interaction, these approaches fail to generate physically consistent trajectories over long time periods, and they do not explicitly address the complex dynamics that arise at traffic intersections which is a critical location in urban networks. Inspired by the World Model paradigm, we have developed an actor centric generative model using transformer-based architecture that is able to capture the actor-actor interaction, at the same time understanding the geometry to the traffic intersection to generate physically grounded trajectories that are based on learned behavior. Moreover, we test the model in a live ``simulation-in-the-loop" setting, where we generate the initial conditions of the actors using SUMO and then let the model control the dynamics of the actors. We let the simulation run for 40000 timesteps (4000 seconds), testing the performance of the model on long timerange and evaluating the trajectories on traffic engineering related metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively captures complex actor-actor interactions and generates long-horizon, physically consistent trajectories, while requiring significantly fewer training samples than traditional agent-centric generative approaches. Our model is able to outperform the baseline in traffic related as well as aggregate metrics where our model beats the baseline by more than 10x on the KL-Divergence.
tldr: This paper introduces "Enactor," a transformer-based, actor-centric generative model that addresses the limitations of existing traffic microsimulation methods by generating long-horizon, physically consistent trajectories via learned behavior, outperforming baselines in traffic-related metrics.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为 "Enactor" 的基于Transformer的，以人为中心的生成模型， 通过学习行为产生长时程、物理一致的轨迹，解决了现有交通微观仿真方法的局限性，并在交通相关指标上优于基线模型。
summary_zh: 交通微观模拟器被广泛用于评估各种“假设”条件下道路网络的性能。然而，控制参与者行为的模型过于简单，无法捕捉到真实的参与者间互动。基于深度学习的方法已被应用于将车辆和行人建模为对周围“环境”（包括车道、信号和相邻参与者）做出响应的“智能体”。尽管这些方法在学习参与者间互动方面有效，但它们无法在长时间内生成物理上一致的轨迹，并且没有明确解决城市网络关键位置--交通路口所产生的复杂动态。受到世界模型范式的启发，我们开发了一种以参与者为中心的生成模型，该模型使用基于Transformer的架构，既能够捕捉参与者间的互动，同时理解交通路口的几何结构，从而生成基于学习行为的、物理上可靠的轨迹。此外，我们在实时“环路仿真”环境中测试了该模型，其中我们使用SUMO生成参与者的初始条件，然后让该模型控制参与者的动态。我们让仿真运行了40000个时间步（4000秒），测试模型在长时间范围内的性能，并评估交通工程相关指标上的轨迹。实验结果表明，所提出的框架有效地捕捉了复杂的参与者间互动，并生成了长时程、物理上一致的轨迹，同时，与传统的以智能体为中心的生成方法相比，需要的训练样本明显更少。我们的模型在交通相关和聚合指标方面都优于基线模型，其中我们的模型在KL散度方面比基线模型高出10倍以上。

title: VLM-AutoDrive: Post-Training Vision-Language Models for Safety-Critical Autonomous Driving Events
summary:
The rapid growth of ego-centric dashcam footage presents a major challenge for detecting safety-critical events such as collisions and near-collisions, scenarios that are brief, rare, and difficult for generic vision models to capture. While multimodal large language models (MLLMs) demonstrate strong general reasoning ability, they underperform in driving contexts due to domain and temporal misalignment.
  We introduce VLM-AutoDrive, a modular post-training framework for adapting pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to high-fidelity anomaly detection. The framework integrates metadata-derived captions, LLM-generated descriptions, visual question answering (VQA) pairs, and chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning supervision to enable domain-aligned and interpretable learning. Off-the-shelf VLMs such as NVIDIA's Cosmos-Reason1 7B (CR1) exhibit near-zero Collision recall in zero-shot settings; fine-tuning with VLM-AutoDrive improves Collision F1 from 0.00 to 0.69 and overall accuracy from 35.35% to 77.27%.
  VLM-AutoDrive offers a scalable recipe for adapting general-purpose VLMs to safety-critical, temporally localized perception tasks. Evaluated on real-world Nexar dashcam videos, it achieves substantial gains in Collision and Near-Collision detection while producing interpretable reasoning traces, bridging the gap between perception, causality, and decision reasoning in autonomous driving.
tldr: VLM-AutoDrive is a post-training framework that adapts VLMs to detect safety-critical autonomous driving events using metadata-derived captions, LLM-generated descriptions, VQA pairs, and CoT reasoning, significantly improving collision and near-collision detection. The framework shows improved performance using NVIDIA's Cosmos-Reason1 7B (CR1) when evaluated on real-world Nexar dashcam videos.
tldr_zh: VLM-AutoDrive是一个后训练框架，它使用元数据衍生的标题、LLM生成的描述、VQA对和CoT推理来调整VLM，以检测安全关键的自动驾驶事件，从而显着提高碰撞和近碰撞的检测。该框架在使用NVIDIA的Cosmos-Reason1 7B (CR1)时，在真实世界的Nexar行车记录仪视频上进行评估时显示出改进的性能。
summary_zh:
第一人称视角行车记录仪视频的快速增长给检测安全关键事件带来了巨大挑战，例如碰撞和近碰撞，这些场景是短暂、罕见且通用视觉模型难以捕捉的。虽然多模态大型语言模型(MLLM)展现出强大的通用推理能力，但由于领域和时间错位，它们在驾驶场景中的表现不佳。

我们提出了VLM-AutoDrive，一个模块化的后训练框架，用于将预训练的视觉-语言模型(VLM)适配到高保真异常检测。该框架集成了元数据导出的描述、LLM生成的描述、视觉问答(VQA)对以及思维链(CoT)推理监督，从而实现领域对齐和可解释的学习。例如，NVIDIA的Cosmos-Reason1 7B (CR1)等现成的VLMs在零样本设置下表现出接近于零的碰撞召回率；使用VLM-AutoDrive进行微调可以将碰撞F1值从0.00提高到0.69，整体准确率从35.35%提高到77.27%。

VLM-AutoDrive为将通用VLMs适配到安全关键、时间局部化的感知任务提供了一种可扩展的方法。在真实世界的Nexar行车记录仪视频上进行的评估表明，它在碰撞和近碰撞检测方面取得了显著的提升，同时产生了可解释的推理过程，从而弥合了自动驾驶中感知、因果关系和决策推理之间的差距。

title: Bridging Semantic and Kinematic Conditions with Diffusion-based Discrete Motion Tokenizer
summary: Prior motion generation largely follows two paradigms: continuous diffusion models that excel at kinematic control, and discrete token-based generators that are effective for semantic conditioning. To combine their strengths, we propose a three-stage framework comprising condition feature extraction (Perception), discrete token generation (Planning), and diffusion-based motion synthesis (Control). Central to this framework is MoTok, a diffusion-based discrete motion tokenizer that decouples semantic abstraction from fine-grained reconstruction by delegating motion recovery to a diffusion decoder, enabling compact single-layer tokens while preserving motion fidelity. For kinematic conditions, coarse constraints guide token generation during planning, while fine-grained constraints are enforced during control through diffusion-based optimization. This design prevents kinematic details from disrupting semantic token planning. On HumanML3D, our method significantly improves controllability and fidelity over MaskControl while using only one-sixth of the tokens, reducing trajectory error from 0.72 cm to 0.08 cm and FID from 0.083 to 0.029. Unlike prior methods that degrade under stronger kinematic constraints, ours improves fidelity, reducing FID from 0.033 to 0.014.
tldr: This paper introduces MoTok, a three-stage framework combining discrete token-based generators for semantic conditioning and continuous diffusion models for kinematic control in motion generation, achieving significantly improved controllability and fidelity with fewer tokens.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为MoTok的三阶段框架，该框架结合了用于语义调节的离散令牌生成器和用于运动学控制的连续扩散模型，在运动生成中实现了显著提高的可控性和保真度，同时减少了令牌数量。
summary_zh: 以往的运动生成主要遵循两种范式：擅长运动学控制的连续扩散模型，以及在语义控制方面有效的基于离散令牌的生成器。为了结合它们的优势，我们提出了一个三阶段框架，包括条件特征提取（感知）、离散令牌生成（规划）和基于扩散的运动合成（控制）。该框架的核心是MoTok，一种基于扩散的离散运动令牌化器，它将语义抽象与精细重建解耦，通过将运动恢复委派给扩散解码器来实现，从而在保持运动保真度的同时实现紧凑的单层令牌。对于运动学条件，粗略约束在规划期间指导令牌生成，而精细约束则在控制期间通过基于扩散的优化来执行。这种设计避免了运动学细节干扰语义令牌规划。在HumanML3D上，我们的方法显著提高了可控性和保真度，优于MaskControl，同时仅使用六分之一的令牌，将轨迹误差从0.72厘米降低到0.08厘米，FID从0.083降低到0.029。与在更强的运动学约束下性能下降的先前方法不同，我们的方法提高了保真度，使FID从0.033降低到0.014。

title: DriveTok: 3D Driving Scene Tokenization for Unified Multi-View Reconstruction and Understanding
summary: With the growing adoption of vision-language-action models and world models in autonomous driving systems, scalable image tokenization becomes crucial as the interface for the visual modality. However, most existing tokenizers are designed for monocular and 2D scenes, leading to inefficiency and inter-view inconsistency when applied to high-resolution multi-view driving scenes. To address this, we propose DriveTok, an efficient 3D driving scene tokenizer for unified multi-view reconstruction and understanding. DriveTok first obtains semantically rich visual features from vision foundation models and then transforms them into the scene tokens with 3D deformable cross-attention. For decoding, we employ a multi-view transformer to reconstruct multi-view features from the scene tokens and use multiple heads to obtain RGB, depth, and semantic reconstructions. We also add a 3D head directly on the scene tokens for 3D semantic occupancy prediction for better spatial awareness. With the multiple training objectives, DriveTok learns unified scene tokens that integrate semantic, geometric, and textural information for efficient multi-view tokenization. Extensive experiments on the widely used nuScenes dataset demonstrate that the scene tokens from DriveTok perform well on image reconstruction, semantic segmentation, depth prediction, and 3D occupancy prediction tasks.
tldr: DriveTok introduces an efficient 3D driving scene tokenizer using deformable cross-attention and multi-view transformers to create unified scene tokens for multi-view reconstruction and 3D scene understanding in autonomous driving.
tldr_zh: DriveTok 提出了一种高效的 3D 驾驶场景 Tokenizer，它使用可变形交叉注意力和多视图 Transformer 来创建统一的场景 Token，用于自动驾驶中的多视图重建和 3D 场景理解。
summary_zh: 随着视觉-语言-动作模型和世界模型在自动驾驶系统中的日益普及，可扩展的图像令牌化作为视觉模态的接口变得至关重要。然而，大多数现有的令牌器是为单目和2D场景设计的，当应用于高分辨率多视角驾驶场景时，会导致效率低下和视角间不一致。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了DriveTok，一种高效的3D驾驶场景令牌器，用于统一的多视角重建和理解。DriveTok首先从视觉基础模型中获取语义丰富的视觉特征，然后通过3D可变形交叉注意力将它们转换成场景令牌。对于解码，我们采用多视角Transformer从场景令牌中重建多视角特征，并使用多个头来获得RGB、深度和语义重建。我们还在场景令牌上直接添加一个3D头，用于3D语义占用预测，以实现更好的空间感知。通过多个训练目标，DriveTok学习统一的场景令牌，整合语义、几何和纹理信息，以实现高效的多视角令牌化。在广泛使用的nuScenes数据集上的大量实验表明，来自DriveTok的场景令牌在图像重建、语义分割、深度预测和3D占用预测任务中表现良好。

title: TAU-R1: Visual Language Model for Traffic Anomaly Understanding
summary: Traffic Anomaly Understanding (TAU) is important for traffic safety in Intelligent Transportation Systems. Recent vision-language models (VLMs) have shown strong capabilities in video understanding. However, progress on TAU remains limited due to the lack of benchmarks and task-specific methodologies. To address this limitation, we introduce Roundabout-TAU, a dataset constructed from real-world roundabout videos collected in collaboration with the City of Carmel, Indiana. The dataset contains 342 clips and is annotated with more than 2,000 question-answer pairs covering multiple aspects of traffic anomaly understanding. Building on this benchmark, we propose TAU-R1, a two-layer vision-language framework for TAU. The first layer is a lightweight anomaly classifier that performs coarse anomaly categorisation, while the second layer is a larger anomaly reasoner that generates detailed event summaries. To improve task-specific reasoning, we introduce a two-stage training strategy consisting of decomposed-QA-enhanced supervised fine-tuning followed by TAU-GRPO, a GRPO-based post-training method with TAU-specific reward functions. Experimental results show that TAU-R1 achieves strong performance on both anomaly classification and reasoning tasks while maintaining deployment efficiency. The dataset and code are available at: https://github.com/siri-rouser/TAU-R1
tldr: The paper introduces a new dataset (Roundabout-TAU) and a vision-language model (TAU-R1) for traffic anomaly understanding in roundabouts, demonstrating strong performance in anomaly classification and reasoning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个新的数据集 (Roundabout-TAU) 和一个视觉语言模型 (TAU-R1)，用于理解环岛中的交通异常，并在异常分类和推理方面表现出强大的性能。
summary_zh: 交通异常理解(TAU)对于智能交通系统中的交通安全至关重要。 近年来，视觉-语言模型(VLM)在视频理解方面表现出强大的能力。 然而，由于缺乏基准和特定于任务的方法，TAU的进展仍然有限。 为了解决这个限制，我们引入了Roundabout-TAU，一个从真实世界环岛视频构建的数据集，该数据集与印第安纳州卡梅尔市合作收集得到。 该数据集包含342个片段，并标注了超过2000个问答对，涵盖了交通异常理解的多个方面。 在此基准的基础上，我们提出了TAU-R1，一个用于TAU的两层视觉-语言框架。 第一层是一个轻量级的异常分类器，执行粗粒度的异常分类，而第二层是一个更大的异常推理器，生成详细的事件摘要。 为了提高特定于任务的推理能力，我们引入了一个两阶段训练策略，包括分解式问答增强的监督微调，以及随后基于GRPO的后训练方法TAU-GRPO，其中包含TAU特定的奖励函数。 实验结果表明，TAU-R1在异常分类和推理任务上都取得了优异的性能，同时保持了部署效率。 数据集和代码可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/siri-rouser/TAU-R1

title: TerraScope: Pixel-Grounded Visual Reasoning for Earth Observation
summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) have shown promise in earth observation (EO), yet they struggle with tasks that require grounding complex spatial reasoning in precise pixel-level visual representations. To address this problem, we introduce TerraScope, a unified VLM that delivers pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning with two key capabilities: (1) modality-flexible reasoning: it handles single-modality inputs (optical or SAR) and adaptively fuses different modalities into the reasoning process when both are available; (2) multi-temporal reasoning: it integrates temporal sequences for change analysis across multiple time points. In addition, we curate Terra-CoT, a large-scale dataset containing 1 million samples with pixel-level masks embedded in reasoning chains across multiple sources. We also propose TerraScope-Bench, the first benchmark for pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning with six sub-tasks that evaluates both answer accuracy and mask quality to ensure authentic pixel-grounded reasoning. Experiments show that TerraScope significantly outperforms existing VLMs on pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning while providing interpretable visual evidence.
tldr: TerraScope is a new VLM for pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning in Earth Observation, featuring modality-flexible and multi-temporal reasoning, a large-scale dataset (Terra-CoT), and a benchmark (TerraScope-Bench). It outperforms existing VLMs in experiments.
tldr_zh: TerraScope是一个新的VLM，用于地球观测中的像素级地理空间推理，具有模态灵活和多时序推理能力，以及一个大型数据集（Terra-CoT）和一个基准（TerraScope-Bench）。实验表明，它优于现有的VLMs。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型(VLMs)在地球观测(EO)领域展现出潜力，但它们在需要将复杂空间推理定位于精确像素级视觉表示的任务中表现不佳。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了TerraScope，一个统一的VLM，它提供像素级地理空间推理，具有两个关键能力：(1)模态灵活推理：它可以处理单模态输入（光学或SAR），并在两者都可用时自适应地将不同的模态融合到推理过程中；(2)多时相推理：它集成时间序列，用于跨多个时间点的变化分析。此外，我们整理了Terra-CoT，一个包含100万样本的大规模数据集，其中像素级掩码嵌入在跨多个来源的推理链中。我们还提出了TerraScope-Bench，第一个用于像素级地理空间推理的基准，包含六个子任务，评估答案准确性和掩码质量，以确保真实的像素级推理。实验表明，TerraScope在像素级地理空间推理方面明显优于现有的VLMs，同时提供可解释的视觉证据。

title: CausalVAD: De-confounding End-to-End Autonomous Driving via Causal Intervention
summary: Planning-oriented end-to-end driving models show great promise, yet they fundamentally learn statistical correlations instead of true causal relationships. This vulnerability leads to causal confusion, where models exploit dataset biases as shortcuts, critically harming their reliability and safety in complex scenarios. To address this, we introduce CausalVAD, a de-confounding training framework that leverages causal intervention. At its core, we design the sparse causal intervention scheme (SCIS), a lightweight, plug-and-play module to instantiate the backdoor adjustment theory in neural networks. SCIS constructs a dictionary of prototypes representing latent driving contexts. It then uses this dictionary to intervene on the model's sparse vectorized queries. This step actively eliminates spurious associations induced by confounders, thereby eliminating spurious factors from the representations for downstream tasks. Extensive experiments on benchmarks like nuScenes show CausalVAD achieves state-of-the-art planning accuracy and safety. Furthermore, our method demonstrates superior robustness against both data bias and noisy scenarios configured to induce causal confusion.
tldr: The paper introduces CausalVAD, a de-confounding training framework utilizing causal intervention via Sparse Causal Intervention Scheme (SCIS) to improve the robustness and safety of end-to-end autonomous driving models by mitigating causal confusion.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了CausalVAD，一个利用稀疏因果干预方案（SCIS）进行因果干预的解混训练框架，通过减轻因果混淆来提高端到端自动驾驶模型的鲁棒性和安全性。
summary_zh: 面向规划的端到端驾驶模型展现出巨大的潜力，但其本质上学习的是统计相关性，而非真正的因果关系。这种脆弱性导致了因果混淆，模型会利用数据集偏差作为捷径，严重损害了其在复杂场景中的可靠性和安全性。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了CausalVAD，一个利用因果干预的去混淆训练框架。其核心在于，我们设计了稀疏因果干预方案（SCIS），一个轻量级、即插即用的模块，用于在神经网络中实例化后门调整理论。SCIS构建了一个代表潜在驾驶环境的prototype字典。然后，它使用该字典对模型中的稀疏向量化查询进行干预。这一步骤主动消除了由混杂因素引起的虚假关联，从而消除了表示中影响下游任务的虚假因素。在nuScenes等基准测试上的大量实验表明，CausalVAD实现了最先进的规划精度和安全性。此外，我们的方法还展示出对于被设计用于诱导因果混淆的数据偏差和噪声场景的卓越鲁棒性。

title: 3DreamBooth: High-Fidelity 3D Subject-Driven Video Generation Model
summary: Creating dynamic, view-consistent videos of customized subjects is highly sought after for a wide range of emerging applications, including immersive VR/AR, virtual production, and next-generation e-commerce. However, despite rapid progress in subject-driven video generation, existing methods predominantly treat subjects as 2D entities, focusing on transferring identity through single-view visual features or textual prompts. Because real-world subjects are inherently 3D, applying these 2D-centric approaches to 3D object customization reveals a fundamental limitation: they lack the comprehensive spatial priors necessary to reconstruct the 3D geometry. Consequently, when synthesizing novel views, they must rely on generating plausible but arbitrary details for unseen regions, rather than preserving the true 3D identity. Achieving genuine 3D-aware customization remains challenging due to the scarcity of multi-view video datasets. While one might attempt to fine-tune models on limited video sequences, this often leads to temporal overfitting. To resolve these issues, we introduce a novel framework for 3D-aware video customization, comprising 3DreamBooth and 3Dapter. 3DreamBooth decouples spatial geometry from temporal motion through a 1-frame optimization paradigm. By restricting updates to spatial representations, it effectively bakes a robust 3D prior into the model without the need for exhaustive video-based training. To enhance fine-grained textures and accelerate convergence, we incorporate 3Dapter, a visual conditioning module. Following single-view pre-training, 3Dapter undergoes multi-view joint optimization with the main generation branch via an asymmetrical conditioning strategy. This design allows the module to act as a dynamic selective router, querying view-specific geometric hints from a minimal reference set. Project page: https://ko-lani.github.io/3DreamBooth/
tldr: The paper introduces 3DreamBooth and 3Dapter to generate view-consistent 3D subject-driven videos from limited data by decoupling spatial geometry from temporal motion and using a visual conditioning module for fine-grained textures.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了3DreamBooth和3Dapter，通过将空间几何与时间运动分离，并使用视觉条件模块来处理精细纹理，从而从有限的数据生成具有视图一致性的3D主题驱动视频。
summary_zh: 为定制化主体创建动态且视角一致的视频，在包括沉浸式VR/AR、虚拟制作和下一代电子商务等新兴应用中具有极高的需求。然而，尽管主体驱动的视频生成技术取得了快速进展，但现有方法主要将主体视为二维实体，侧重于通过单视点视觉特征或文本提示来传递身份。由于真实世界的主体本质上是三维的，将这些以二维为中心的方法应用于三维物体定制会暴露出一个根本性的局限：它们缺乏重建三维几何所需的全面空间先验。因此，在合成新视角时，它们必须依赖于为未见区域生成似是而非但任意的细节，而不是保留真实的3D身份。由于多视角视频数据集的稀缺性，实现真正的3D感知定制仍然具有挑战性。虽然可以在有限的视频序列上微调模型，但这通常会导致时间过拟合。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一个用于3D感知视频定制的新框架，包含3DreamBooth和3Dapter。3DreamBooth通过单帧优化范式将空间几何与时间运动解耦。通过限制对空间表示的更新，它有效地将鲁棒的3D先验烘焙到模型中，而无需进行详尽的基于视频的训练。为了增强精细纹理并加速收敛，我们引入了3Dapter，一个视觉条件模块。在单视点预训练之后，3Dapter通过非对称条件策略与主生成分支进行多视点联合优化。这种设计允许该模块作为一个动态选择性路由器，从最小的参考集中查询特定视角的几何提示。项目页面：https://ko-lani.github.io/3DreamBooth/

title: OnlinePG: Online Open-Vocabulary Panoptic Mapping with 3D Gaussian Splatting
summary: Open-vocabulary scene understanding with online panoptic mapping is essential for embodied applications to perceive and interact with environments. However, existing methods are predominantly offline or lack instance-level understanding, limiting their applicability to real-world robotic tasks. In this paper, we propose OnlinePG, a novel and effective system that integrates geometric reconstruction and open-vocabulary perception using 3D Gaussian Splatting in an online setting. Technically, to achieve online panoptic mapping, we employ an efficient local-to-global paradigm with a sliding window. To build local consistency map, we construct a 3D segment clustering graph that jointly leverages geometric and semantic cues, fusing inconsistent segments within sliding window into complete instances. Subsequently, to update the global map, we construct explicit grids with spatial attributes for the local 3D Gaussian map and fuse them into the global map via robust bidirectional bipartite 3D Gaussian instance matching. Finally, we utilize the fused VLM features inside the 3D spatial attribute grids to achieve open-vocabulary scene understanding. Extensive experiments on widely used datasets demonstrate that our method achieves better performance among online approaches, while maintaining real-time efficiency.
tldr: This paper introduces OnlinePG, a real-time system for online open-vocabulary panoptic mapping using 3D Gaussian Splatting, enabling embodied agents to perceive and interact with environments with instance-level understanding.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了OnlinePG，一个使用3D高斯溅射进行在线开放词汇全景地图构建的实时系统，使具身智能体能够感知环境并进行交互，并具有实例级别的理解。
summary_zh: 利用在线全景地图进行开放词汇场景理解对于具身应用感知环境和与之交互至关重要。然而，现有方法主要为离线或缺乏实例级理解，限制了其在现实世界机器人任务中的适用性。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为OnlinePG的新颖有效的系统，该系统在在线环境下使用3D高斯溅射集成了几何重建和开放词汇感知。从技术上讲，为了实现在线全景地图构建，我们采用了一种高效的局部到全局的范式，并配有滑动窗口。为了建立局部一致性图，我们构建了一个3D分割聚类图，该图联合利用了几何和语义线索，将滑动窗口内的不一致分割融合为完整的实例。随后，为了更新全局地图，我们构建具有空间属性的显式网格用于局部3D高斯地图，并通过鲁棒的双向二分3D高斯实例匹配将其融合到全局地图中。最后，我们利用3D空间属性网格内融合的VLM特征来实现开放词汇场景理解。在广泛使用的数据集上的大量实验表明，我们的方法在在线方法中实现了更好的性能，同时保持了实时效率。

title: NymeriaPlus: Enriching Nymeria Dataset with Additional Annotations and Data
summary: The Nymeria Dataset, released in 2024, is a large-scale collection of in-the-wild human activities captured with multiple egocentric wearable devices that are spatially localized and temporally synchronized. It provides body-motion ground truth recorded with a motion-capture suit, device trajectories, semi-dense 3D point clouds, and in-context narrations. In this paper, we upgrade Nymeria and introduce NymeriaPlus. NymeriaPlus features: (1) improved human motion in Momentum Human Rig (MHR) and SMPL formats; (2) dense 3D and 2D bounding box annotations for indoor objects and structural elements; (3) instance-level 3D object reconstructions; and (4) additional modalities e.g., basemap recordings, audio, and wristband videos. By consolidating these complementary modalities and annotations into a single, coherent benchmark, NymeriaPlus strengthens Nymeria into a more powerful in-the-wild egocentric dataset. We expect NymeriaPlus to bridge a key gap in existing egocentric resources and to support a broader range of research, including unique explorations of multimodal learning for embodied AI.
tldr: NymeriaPlus is an enriched version of the Nymeria egocentric dataset, featuring improved human motion capture, dense 3D and 2D object annotations, instance-level 3D object reconstructions, and additional modalities like audio and wristband videos to support multimodal embodied AI research.
tldr_zh: NymeriaPlus是对Nymeria第一人称视角数据集的升级版，它改进了人体动作捕捉，增加了密集的3D和2D物体标注、实例级别的3D物体重建，以及音频和腕带视频等更多模态的数据，旨在支持多模态具身智能研究。
summary_zh: Nymeria数据集于2024年发布，是一个大规模的真实场景下人类活动数据集，通过多个空间定位和时间同步的以自我为中心的穿戴设备捕获。它提供由动作捕捉服记录的身体运动真值、设备轨迹、半稠密3D点云以及上下文叙述。在本文中，我们升级了Nymeria并推出了NymeriaPlus。NymeriaPlus具有以下特点：（1）改进了Momentum Human Rig (MHR)和SMPL格式的人体运动；（2）室内物体和结构元素的稠密3D和2D bounding box标注；（3）实例级别的3D物体重建；（4）额外的模态，例如基础地图记录、音频和腕带视频。通过将这些互补的模态和标注整合到一个单一、连贯的基准中，NymeriaPlus增强了Nymeria，使其成为一个更强大的真实场景下以自我为中心的数据集。我们期望NymeriaPlus能够弥合现有以自我为中心的资源中的一个关键差距，并支持更广泛的研究，包括对具身人工智能的多模态学习的独特探索。

title: SR-Nav: Spatial Relationships Matter for Zero-shot Object Goal Navigation
summary: Zero-shot object-goal navigation aims to find target objects in unseen environments using only egocentric observation. Recent methods leverage foundation models' comprehension and reasoning capabilities to enhance navigation performance. However, when faced with poor viewpoints or weak semantic cues, foundation models often fail to support reliable reasoning in both perception and planning, resulting in inefficient or failed navigation. We observe that inherent relationships among objects and regions encode structured scene priors, which help agents infer plausible target locations even under partial observations. Motivated by this insight, we propose Spatial Relation-aware Navigation (SR-Nav), a framework that models both observed and experience-based spatial relationships to enhance both perception and planning. Specifically, SR-Nav first constructs a Dynamic Spatial Relationship Graph (DSRG) that encodes the target-centered spatial relationships through the foundation models and updates dynamically with real-time observations. We then introduce a Relation-aware Matching Module. It utilizes relationship matching instead of naive detection, leveraging diverse relationships in the DSRG to verify and correct errors, enhancing visual perception robustness. Finally, we design a Dynamic Relationship Planning Module to reduce the planning search space by dynamically computing the optimal paths based on the DSRG from the current position, thereby guiding planning and reducing exploration redundancy. Experiments on HM3D show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both success rate and navigation efficiency. The code will be publicly available at https://github.com/Mzyw-1314/SR-Nav
tldr: The paper introduces SR-Nav, a framework for zero-shot object goal navigation that leverages spatial relationships between objects to enhance perception and planning, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance on HM3D.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了SR-Nav，一个用于零样本物体目标导航的框架，它利用物体之间的空间关系来增强感知和规划，并在HM3D上展示了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 零样本物体-目标导航旨在使用仅有的以自我为中心的观测，在未见过的环境中找到目标物体。最近的方法利用了基础模型的理解和推理能力来提升导航性能。然而，当面临不良视角或微弱语义线索时，基础模型往往无法在感知和规划中支持可靠的推理，导致低效或失败的导航。我们观察到，物体和区域之间固有的关系编码了结构化的场景先验知识，这有助于智能体即使在部分观测下也能推断出合理的目标位置。受此启发，我们提出了一种空间关系感知导航（SR-Nav）框架，该框架对观察到的和基于经验的空间关系进行建模，以增强感知和规划。具体来说，SR-Nav首先构建一个动态空间关系图（DSRG），该图通过基础模型编码以目标为中心的空间关系，并随着实时观测动态更新。然后，我们引入了一个关系感知匹配模块，它利用关系匹配而非朴素检测，利用DSRG中的各种关系来验证和纠正错误，从而增强视觉感知的鲁棒性。最后，我们设计了一个动态关系规划模块，通过基于DSRG从当前位置动态计算最优路径来减少规划搜索空间，从而指导规划并减少探索冗余。在HM3D上的实验表明，我们的方法在成功率和导航效率方面都达到了最先进的性能。代码将在https://github.com/Mzyw-1314/SR-Nav上公开提供。

title: CycleCap: Improving VLMs Captioning Performance via Self-Supervised Cycle Consistency Fine-Tuning
summary: Visual-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in image captioning, visual question answering, and visual reasoning. Yet they remain prone to vision-language misalignment, often producing overly generic or hallucinated descriptions. Existing approaches address this via instruction tuning-requiring costly, large-scale annotated datasets or via complex test-time frameworks for caption refinement. In this work, we revisit image-text alignment through the lens of cycle consistency: given an image and a caption generated by an image-to-text model, the backward mapping through a text-to-image model should reconstruct an image that closely matches the original. In our setup, a VLM serves as the image-to-text component, while a pre-trained text-to-image model closes the loop by reconstructing the image from the generated caption. Building on this, we introduce CycleCap, a fine-tuning scheme to improve image captioning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a reward based on the similarity between the original and reconstructed images, computed on-the-fly. Unlike previous work that uses cycle consistency loss for preference dataset construction, our method leverages cycle consistency directly as a self-supervised training signal. This enables the use of raw images alone, eliminating the need for curated image-text datasets, while steering the VLM to produce more accurate and grounded text descriptions. Applied to four VLMs ranging from 1B to 7B parameters, CycleCap yields consistent improvements across captioning and hallucination benchmarks, surpassing state-of-the-art methods that rely on supervised cycle consistency training.
tldr: CycleCap is a self-supervised fine-tuning method that improves VLM image captioning by using cycle consistency between image-to-text and text-to-image models, achieving state-of-the-art results without curated image-text datasets.
tldr_zh: CycleCap 是一种自监督微调方法，它通过图像-文本和文本-图像模型之间的循环一致性来提高 VLM 的图像描述能力，无需策划的图像-文本数据集即可实现最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）在图像描述、视觉问答和视觉推理等方面取得了显著进展。然而，它们仍然容易出现视觉-语言不对齐的问题，经常产生过于泛化或虚构的描述。现有方法通常通过指令调优来解决这个问题——需要昂贵的大规模标注数据集，或者通过复杂的测试时框架进行描述改进。在这项工作中，我们通过循环一致性的视角重新审视图像-文本对齐问题：给定一张图像和一个由图像到文本模型生成的描述，通过文本到图像模型的反向映射应该重建一张与原始图像高度匹配的图像。在我们的设置中，一个VLM充当图像到文本的组件，而一个预训练的文本到图像模型通过从生成的描述重建图像来闭合循环。在此基础上，我们引入了CycleCap，一种微调方案，旨在使用基于群体相对策略优化（GRPO）的训练方法来改进图像描述生成，该方法使用基于原始图像和重建图像之间相似度的奖励信号，该相似度可以实时计算。与先前使用循环一致性损失构建偏好数据集的工作不同，我们的方法直接利用循环一致性作为自监督训练信号。这使得仅使用原始图像成为可能，消除了对人工标注的图像-文本数据集的需求，同时引导VLM生成更准确、更贴近事实的文本描述。应用于参数规模从10亿到70亿的四个VLM后，CycleCap在描述生成和幻觉基准测试中均产生了持续的改进，超越了依赖于监督循环一致性训练的最新方法。

title: Introducing M: A Modular, Modifiable Social Robot
summary: We present M, an open-source, low-cost social robot platform designed to reduce platform friction that slows social robotics research by making robots easier to reproduce, modify, and deploy in real-world settings. M combines a modular mechanical design, multimodal sensing, and expressive yet mechanically simple actuation architecture with a ROS2-native software package that cleanly separates perception, expression control, and data management. The platform includes a simulation environment with interface equivalence to hardware to support rapid sim-to-real transfer of interaction behaviors. We demonstrate extensibility through additional sensing/actuation modules and provide example interaction templates for storytelling and two-way conversational coaching. Finally, we report real-world use in participatory design and week-long in-home deployments, showing how M can serve as a practical foundation for longitudinal, reproducible social robotics research.
tldr: The paper introduces M, an open-source, modular social robot platform with a ROS2-based software package and simulation environment, designed for easier reproduction, modification, and deployment in real-world environments to accelerate social robotics research.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了M，一个开源的、模块化的社交机器人平台，具有基于ROS2的软件包和仿真环境，旨在更容易地在真实环境中进行复制、修改和部署，从而加速社交机器人研究。
summary_zh: 我们提出了 M，一个开源、低成本的社交机器人平台，旨在降低阻碍社交机器人研究的平台摩擦，使得机器人更容易复制、修改和部署在真实世界环境中。M 结合了模块化的机械设计、多模态感知、以及具有表现力且机械结构简单的驱动架构，同时配备了原生 ROS2 软件包，该软件包清晰地分离了感知、表达控制和数据管理。 该平台包含一个仿真环境，其接口与硬件等效，以支持交互行为的快速从仿真到真实的转换。 我们通过额外的传感/驱动模块展示了其可扩展性，并提供了讲故事和双向会话指导的示例交互模板。 最后，我们报告了在参与式设计和为期一周的家庭部署中的真实世界应用，展示了 M 如何成为纵向、可重复的社交机器人研究的实际基础。

title: DROID-SLAM in the Wild
summary: We present a robust, real-time RGB SLAM system that handles dynamic environments by leveraging differentiable Uncertainty-aware Bundle Adjustment. Traditional SLAM methods typically assume static scenes, leading to tracking failures in the presence of motion. Recent dynamic SLAM approaches attempt to address this challenge using predefined dynamic priors or uncertainty-aware mapping, but they remain limited when confronted with unknown dynamic objects or highly cluttered scenes where geometric mapping becomes unreliable. In contrast, our method estimates per-pixel uncertainty by exploiting multi-view visual feature inconsistency, enabling robust tracking and reconstruction even in real-world environments. The proposed system achieves state-of-the-art camera poses and scene geometry in cluttered dynamic scenarios while running in real time at around 10 FPS. Code and datasets are available at https://github.com/MoyangLi00/DROID-W.git.
tldr: DROID-SLAM in the Wild introduces a real-time RGB SLAM system leveraging differentiable Uncertainty-aware Bundle Adjustment for robust performance in dynamic and cluttered environments, outperforming existing methods in pose estimation and scene geometry.
tldr_zh: DROID-SLAM in the Wild 提出了一个实时的RGB SLAM系统，利用可微分的不确定性感知束调整，在动态和杂乱的环境中实现了稳健的性能，并在姿态估计和场景几何方面优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种鲁棒的、实时的RGB SLAM系统，该系统通过利用可微分的、不确定性感知的光束法平差来处理动态环境。传统的SLAM方法通常假设场景是静态的，这会导致在存在运动的情况下跟踪失败。最近的动态SLAM方法尝试使用预定义的动态先验或不确定性感知的地图构建来解决这一挑战，但当面对未知的动态物体或几何映射变得不可靠的高度杂乱场景时，它们仍然受到限制。相比之下，我们的方法通过利用多视图视觉特征的不一致性来估计每个像素的不确定性，从而即使在现实世界环境中也能实现鲁棒的跟踪和重建。所提出的系统在杂乱的动态场景中实现了最先进的相机姿态和场景几何，同时以大约10 FPS的速度实时运行。代码和数据集可在https://github.com/MoyangLi00/DROID-W.git上获取。

title: CAMO: A Conditional Neural Solver for the Multi-objective Multiple Traveling Salesman Problem
summary: Robotic systems often require a team of robots to collectively visit multiple targets while optimizing competing objectives, such as total travel cost and makespan. This setting can be formulated as the Multi-Objective Multiple Traveling Salesman Problem (MOMTSP). Although learning-based methods have shown strong performance on the single-agent TSP and multi-objective TSP variants, they rarely address the combined challenges of multi-agent coordination and multi-objective trade-offs, which introduce dual sources of complexity. To bridge this gap, we propose CAMO, a conditional neural solver for MOMTSP that generalizes across varying numbers of targets, agents, and preference vectors, and yields high-quality approximations to the Pareto front (PF). Specifically, CAMO consists of a conditional encoder to fuse preferences into instance representations, enabling explicit control over multi-objective trade-offs, and a collaborative decoder that coordinates all agents by alternating agent selection and node selection to construct multi-agent tours autoregressively. To further improve generalization, we train CAMO with a REINFORCE-based objective over a mixed distribution of problem sizes. Extensive experiments show that CAMO outperforms both neural and conventional heuristics, achieving a closer approximation of PFs. In addition, ablation results validate the contributions of CAMO's key components, and real-world tests on a mobile robot platform demonstrate its practical applicability.
tldr: The paper introduces CAMO, a conditional neural solver for the Multi-Objective Multiple Traveling Salesman Problem (MOMTSP), which uses a conditional encoder and collaborative decoder to optimize multi-agent tours with competing objectives, demonstrating improved performance over existing methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种用于多目标多旅行商问题（MOMTSP）的条件神经求解器CAMO，它使用条件编码器和协作解码器来优化具有竞争目标的多智能体路径，并展示了优于现有方法的性能。
summary_zh: 机器人系统通常需要一组机器人集体访问多个目标点，同时优化相互竞争的目标，例如总旅行成本和完工时间。这种场景可以被形式化为多目标多旅行商问题（MOMTSP）。虽然基于学习的方法在单智能体TSP和多目标TSP变体上表现出强大的性能，但它们很少解决多智能体协调和多目标权衡的双重挑战，这引入了双重复杂性来源。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出CAMO，一种用于MOMTSP的条件神经求解器，它可以推广到不同数量的目标点、智能体和偏好向量，并产生对帕累托前沿（PF）的高质量近似。具体来说，CAMO包含一个条件编码器，用于将偏好融合到实例表示中，从而能够显式控制多目标权衡；以及一个协作解码器，通过交替进行智能体选择和节点选择来协调所有智能体，从而以自回归的方式构建多智能体路线。为了进一步提高泛化能力，我们使用基于REINFORCE的目标函数，在一个混合的问题规模分布上训练CAMO。大量的实验表明，CAMO优于神经和传统的启发式方法，实现了对PF的更接近近似。此外，消融实验验证了CAMO关键组件的贡献，在移动机器人平台上的真实世界测试证明了它的实际适用性。

title: Fire as a Service: Augmenting Robot Simulators with Thermally and Visually Accurate Fire Dynamics
summary: Most existing robot simulators prioritize rigid-body dynamics and photorealistic rendering, but largely neglect the thermally and optically complex phenomena that characterize real-world fire environments. For robots envisioned as future firefighters, this limitation hinders both reliable capability evaluation and the generation of representative training data prior to deployment in hazardous scenarios. To address these challenges, we introduce Fire as a Service (FaaS), a novel, asynchronous co-simulation framework that augments existing robot simulators with high-fidelity and computationally efficient fire simulations. Our pipeline enables robots to experience accurate, multi-species thermodynamic heat transfer and visually consistent volumetric smoke without disrupting high-frequency rigid-body control loops.
